Slot Reconciliation Job (`SlotReconciliationJob`)\n\n**Owner:** `scheduling` → `@Scheduled`\n\n**Cadence:** `@Scheduled(fixedDelay = \"PT1H\")` — every hour, after last run ends.\n\n**Algorithm:**\n```\nLoad all ShowSlot where status = ACTIVE AND eb_event_id IS NOT NULL:\nfor each slot:\n    try:\n        GET /events/{eb_event_id}/\n        if response not found (404) OR status in [CANCELLED, DELETED]:\n            slot.lastSyncError = \"EB mismatch: EB event status=\" + ebStatus + \" but internal=ACTIVE\"\n            slot.lastAttemptedAt = now\n            (do NOT auto-change internal status — flag only)\n            persist slot\n            log WARN: \"Slot {} eb_event {} mismatch: internal=ACTIVE, EB={}\"\n        else if response.status == LIVE:\n            // all good — no action\n    catch EbAuthException:\n        log ERROR: \"Reconciliation aborted for slot {}: EB auth failure\"\n        break inner loop — no point retrying all slots if token is expired\n    catch Exception:\n        log WARN: \"Reconciliation check failed for slot {}: {}\"\n        continue to next slot\n```\n\n**Flag-not-fix policy:** Mismatches are flagged via `lastSyncError`. The reconciliation job NEVER auto-transitions an ACTIVE slot to CANCELLED. Admin sees it in `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/mismatches`.\n\n**Scheduler failure handling:** Outer `try-catch(Throwable)` at job level catches anything not caught by the inner loop. Logs error. Scheduler thread continues — subsequent scheduled runs execute normally.\n\n---\n\n### 21. Flow 1 × Flow 2 Cache Integration Summary\n\nThis section consolidates how Flow 2 state changes interact with the Flow 1 snapshot cache (defined in SPEC_1 Stage 6).\n\n| Flow 2 Event | Who handles it | Cache effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| Venue CREATED | `VenueService.createVenue()` | None — no event_catalog row yet |\n| Venue UPDATED | `VenueService.updateVenue()` | `snapshotCache.invalidate(orgId, cityId)` — venue name/address in event cards |\n| Slot → ACTIVE | `SlotActivatedListener` (from `ShowSlotActivatedEvent`) | `EventCatalogSyncService.sync()` creates catalog row. Then `snapshotCache.invalidate(orgId, cityId)`. |\n| Slot → CANCELLED | `SlotCancelledListener` (from `ShowSlotCancelledEvent`) | Sets `event_catalog.state = CANCELLED`. Then `snapshotCache.invalidate(orgId, cityId)`. |\n| EB webhook (event.updated) | `EventbriteWebhookEventListener` (Flow 1, existing) | Invalidates cache if existing row found. Does NOT create new rows. |\n| VenueReconciliation flags drift | `VenueDriftDetectedEvent` published | No direct cache effect — admin resolves, then venue update triggers invalidation |\n\n**Read path (from Flow 1, unchanged):** `GET /api/v1/catalog/events?cityId=…` → check Redis snapshot (`catalog:snapshot:{orgId}:{cityId}`) → on miss, query DB → return with `stale` flag and `source` indicator. ACTIVE EB-synced slots now appear in catalog within seconds of `SlotActivatedListener` running (not up to 4 hours).\n\n\n---\n\n## Stage 7 — Error Handling\n\n> **GlobalExceptionHandler contract (Hard Rule #5):** One `@ControllerAdvice` lives in\n> `shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler`. No module declares its own. All HTTP error\n> shapes conform to `ErrorResponse` from `shared/common/dto/`.\n>\n> **Flow 1 baseline:** SPEC_1 Stage 7 defines rules 1–9 for the catalog/webhook path. Those rules\n> remain unchanged and are not repeated here. Flow 2 adds or refines rules for the scheduling\n> path. Cross-references are noted per section.\n\n---\n\n### Key Distinction — Two Failure Counters\n\nFlow 1 and Flow 2 both track consecutive failures, but on **different entities** for **different purposes**:\n\n| Counter | Entity | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `WebhookConfig.consecutiveFailures` | `discovery-catalog` (Flow 1) | Tracks EB catalog sync / webhook delivery failures. Drives `cooldown_until` on the org. |\n| `ShowSlot.syncAttemptCount` | `scheduling` (Flow 2) | Tracks EB event sync failures for a specific slot. Drives admin visibility (`mismatches` list). No cooldown — slot is retry-eligible after every failure. |\n\nThey are independent. A slot failing EB sync does NOT increment `WebhookConfig.consecutiveFailures`. A webhook delivery failure does NOT affect `ShowSlot.syncAttemptCount`.\n\n---\n\n### Exception Hierarchy — New Types Added by Flow 2\n\nAll new exceptions extend existing `shared/` base classes. No new base classes are introduced.\n\n```\nshared/common/exception/\n├── ResourceNotFoundException (404)       ← from Flow 1\n│   └── SchedulingNotFoundException       ← NEW  slot / occurrence / org-auth not found\n│   └── CatalogNotFoundException          ← from Flow 1 (venue not found in discovery-catalog)\n├── BusinessRuleException (422)           ← from shared/ — used in Flow 1; now also used in Flow 2\n│   └── SlotConflictException (422)       ← NEW  carries ConflictAlternativeResponse in payload\n├── ValidationException (400)             ← from Flow 1\n│   └── SlotValidationException           ← NEW  slot/venue request validation failures\n└── SystemIntegrationException (502)      ← from shared/\n    └── EbAuthException                   ← from Flow 1; reused in Flow 2 token refresh path\n    └── EbIntegrationException            ← from Flow 1; reused in Flow 2 EB call failures\n```\n\n**GlobalExceptionHandler additions for Flow 2:**\n\n| Exception | HTTP Status | `errorCode` |\n|---|---|---|\n| `SlotConflictException` | 422 | `SLOT_CONFLICT` |\n| `BusinessRuleException` | 422 | `BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION` |\n| `SlotValidationException` | 400 | `SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR` |\n| `SchedulingNotFoundException` | 404 | `SLOT_NOT_FOUND` / `VENUE_NOT_FOUND` |\n| `EbAuthException` (token path) | 502 | `EB_AUTH_FAILURE` |\n| `EbIntegrationException` (sync path) | 502 | `EB_INTEGRATION_ERROR` |\n\n> `EbAuthException` and `EbIntegrationException` are reused from Flow 1 (`shared/eventbrite/exception/`). They are already mapped in `GlobalExceptionHandler` from Flow 1. No duplicate mapping needed — confirm the handler maps these to 502 with the `errorCode` fields above.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Request Validation Errors (400)\n\n**Applies to:** `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues`, `PUT /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues/{id}`, `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots`, `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}`, `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/submit`\n\n**Validation failures handled by `@Valid` on request DTOs + `GlobalExceptionHandler`:**\n\n| Field | Condition | Message |\n|---|---|---|\n| `name` (venue/slot) | blank or null | `\"name is required\"` |\n| `cityId` (venue) | null | `\"cityId is required\"` |\n| `venueId` (slot) | null | `\"venueId is required\"` |\n| `startTime` / `endTime` | null | `\"startTime and endTime are required\"` |\n| `endTime ≤ startTime` | custom `@ValidTimeRange` | `\"endTime must be after startTime\"` |\n| `capacity` | ≤ 0 | `\"capacity must be greater than 0\"` |\n| `seatingMode` | invalid enum value | `\"seatingMode must be GA or RESERVED\"` |\n| `sourceSeatMapId` | null when `seatingMode = RESERVED` | `\"sourceSeatMapId is required for RESERVED seating\"` |\n| `pricingTiers` | empty list on submit | `\"At least one pricing tier is required\"` |\n| `pricingTier.priceAmount` | < 0 | `\"priceAmount must be non-negative\"` |\n| `pricingTier.quota` | ≤ 0 | `\"quota must be greater than 0\"` |\n| `pricingTier.tierType = FREE` AND `priceAmount > 0` | custom validator | `\"FREE tier must have priceAmount = 0\"` |\n| `currency` | not 3-char ISO 4217 | `\"currency must be a valid ISO 4217 code\"` |\n\n**Error shape:**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR\",\n  \"message\": \"Request validation failed\",\n  \"details\": [\n    { \"field\": \"endTime\", \"message\": \"endTime must be after startTime\" },\n    { \"field\": \"sourceSeatMapId\", \"message\": \"sourceSeatMapId is required for RESERVED seating\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n> **Flow 1 alignment:** Flow 1 (SPEC_1 Stage 7) does not define 400 validation handling explicitly — it defines `InvalidCatalogSearchException` for bad search params. The same `GlobalExceptionHandler` handles both. No conflict: `@MethodArgumentNotValidException` (Spring's bean validation) is already handled by `GlobalExceptionHandler` returning a 400 with field-level `details` array. Flow 2 leverages this same handler — no change needed to the handler itself, only new validator annotations on the new DTOs.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Resource Not Found (404)\n\n**Trigger locations:**\n- `ShowSlotService`: slot not found by `slotId` → `SchedulingNotFoundException(\"Slot not found: \" + slotId)`\n- `ShowSlotService`: occurrence not found → `SchedulingNotFoundException(\"Occurrence not found: \" + occurrenceId)`\n- `VenueService` (discovery-catalog): venue not found by `venueId` → `CatalogNotFoundException(\"Venue not found: \" + venueId)` *(reused from Flow 1)*\n- `OrgOAuthService` / `EbTokenStore`: no `OrganizationAuth` row for `orgId` → `SchedulingNotFoundException(\"No EB auth configured for org: \" + orgId)` *(before attempting EB connection)*\n- `scheduling` REST call to `discovery-catalog` returns 404 → propagated as `SchedulingNotFoundException(\"Venue not found via catalog: \" + venueId)`\n\n**Error shape (standard):**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"SLOT_NOT_FOUND\",\n  \"message\": \"Slot not found: 9999\"\n}\n```\n\n> **Flow 1 alignment:** `CatalogNotFoundException` is already defined and mapped in `GlobalExceptionHandler`. `SchedulingNotFoundException` is a new peer class extending the same `ResourceNotFoundException`. No change to the handler dispatch logic — just a new subclass registered.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Slot Conflict (422)\n\n**Trigger:** `ConflictDetectionService.checkConflicts()` detects overlap or turnaround gap violation.\n\n**Exception:** `SlotConflictException(ConflictAlternativeResponse alternatives)` extends `BusinessRuleException`.\n\n**Error shape:**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"SLOT_CONFLICT\",\n  \"message\": \"A conflicting slot exists at this venue for the requested time window.\",\n  \"alternatives\": {\n    \"sameVenueAlternatives\": [\n      { \"proposedStart\": \"2026-04-15T21:00:00+05:30\", \"proposedEnd\": \"2026-04-16T01:00:00+05:30\" }\n    ],\n    \"nearbyVenueAlternatives\": [\n      { \"venueId\": 12, \"venueName\": \"Jio World Garden\", \"city\": \"Mumbai\", \"capacity\": 5000 }\n    ],\n    \"adjustedTimeOptions\": [\n      { \"proposedStart\": \"2026-04-15T22:00:00+05:30\", \"proposedEnd\": \"2026-04-16T02:00:00+05:30\" }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**GlobalExceptionHandler rule:** Catch `SlotConflictException` → 422 → serialize `exception.getAlternatives()` into `alternatives` field on the `ErrorResponse`.\n\n**Empty alternatives handling:** If `nearbyVenueAlternatives` list is empty (no other synced venues in city), the list is `[]` — not omitted. Frontend renders a \"no alternatives available\" message.\n\n---\n\n### 4. State Machine Violations (422)\n\n**Trigger:** `ShowSlotStateMachineService.sendEvent()` rejects a transition because:\n- Current state does not allow the requested event (e.g., `ACTIVE → DRAFT` via submission)\n- Guard fails (e.g., no pricing tier on SUBMIT, `eb_event_id` null on EB_PUBLISHED guard)\n- Admin attempts to edit a `PENDING_SYNC` or `CANCELLED` slot field that is locked\n\n**Exception:** `BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: {from} → {to}\")` or `BusinessRuleException(\"{specific rule message}\")`\n\n**Common messages:**\n\n| Trigger | Message |\n|---|---|\n| Submit non-DRAFT slot | `\"Only DRAFT slots can be submitted\"` |\n| Submit slot with no pricing tiers | `\"Slot must have at least one pricing tier before submission\"` |\n| Edit PENDING_SYNC slot | `\"Slot cannot be edited while sync is in progress (PENDING_SYNC)\"` |\n| Edit CANCELLED slot | `\"Slot is in a terminal state and cannot be modified\"` |\n| Cancel DRAFT slot | `\"DRAFT slots must be deleted, not cancelled\"` |\n| Change seatingMode on ACTIVE slot | `\"seatingMode cannot be changed after slot is ACTIVE\"` |\n| Change isRecurring / recurrenceRule on ACTIVE slot | `\"Recurrence settings cannot be changed after slot is ACTIVE\"` |\n| ACTIVE → PENDING_SYNC transition | `\"Invalid slot transition: ACTIVE → PENDING_SYNC\"` |\n| CANCELLED → any active state | `\"Invalid slot transition: CANCELLED → {target}\"` |\n\n**Error shape:**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION\",\n  \"message\": \"Only DRAFT slots can be submitted\"\n}\n```\n\n> **Flow 1 alignment:** `BusinessRuleException` → 422 is already defined in `shared/` and mapped in `GlobalExceptionHandler`. Admin retries via `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/retry-sync`.\n\n---\n\n### 8. EB Transient Failures — D.2–D.5 Ticket/Inventory/SeatMap (async, no HTTP)\n\n**Path:** `SlotDraftCreatedListener` → `SlotTicketSyncService` fails on any D.2–D.5 step.\n\n**No HTTP response to admin** (this runs async after `SlotDraftCreatedEvent`). The admin's original `/submit` call already returned `200`.\n\n**Retry policy:** `@Retryable` on each `EbTicketService` / `EbCapacityService` method call — same config as §7. Retry within `SlotTicketSyncService` before publishing failure event.\n\n**After all retries on a step exhausted:**\n```\npublish TicketSyncFailedEvent(slotId, ebEventId, reason=\"D.{step}: {lastException.message}\")\n    ↓\nTicketSyncFailedListener (scheduling):\n    slot.syncAttemptCount += 1\n    slot.lastSyncError = event.reason\n    slot.lastAttemptedAt = now\n    persist slot (status stays PENDING_SYNC)\n    publish SlotSyncFailedEvent(slotId, reason) ← for future admin notification hook\n    log WARN: \"Ticket sync failed for slot={} eb_event={}: {}\"\n```\n\n**Idempotency on retry:** When admin retries via `retry-sync`, `SlotTicketSyncService` checks each `ShowSlotPricingTier.ebTicketClassId` before calling D.2. If already populated → skip that tier's D.2 call. Same for `ebInventoryTierId` before D.3. This prevents duplicate ticket class creation on Eventbrite on partial retries.\n\n**Partial failure atomicity:** There is no EB rollback of completed steps. If D.3 fails after D.2 succeeded, the ticket classes remain on EB. Partial state is visible in `ShowSlotPricingTier` rows.\n\n> **Flow 1 alignment:** Flow 1 (SPEC_1 §7.1) handles EB 5xx in the async catalog refresh path and increments `WebhookConfig.consecutiveFailures`. Flow 2's async failure path increments `ShowSlot.syncAttemptCount` instead. Same pattern, different counters, different entities — no conflict.\n\n---\n\n### 9. EB Transient Failures — D.6 Publish (502 synchronous)\n\n**Path:** `TicketSyncCompletedListener` → `ShowSlotService.onTicketSyncComplete()` → `EbEventSyncService.publishEvent()` fails.\n\n> This runs asynchronously from the admin's `/submit` call but synchronously from the event listener's perspective. The admin sees the slot in `PENDING_SYNC` and checks status.\n\n**Retry policy:** `@Retryable` on `EbEventSyncService.publishEvent()` — same config as §7.\n\n**After all retries exhausted:**\n```\nslot.syncAttemptCount += 1\nslot.lastSyncError = \"D.6 publish failed: \" + lastException.message\nslot.lastAttemptedAt = now\npersist slot (status stays PENDING_SYNC)\nlog WARN: \"Publish failed for slot={} eb_event={}\"\npublish SlotSyncFailedEvent(slotId, reason)\n```\n\n**Critical rule:** The EB draft event (`eb_event_id` is populated) is **NOT re-created** on D.6 failure. On admin retry, `ShowSlotService.retrySync()` sees `eb_event_id != null` and `status = PENDING_SYNC` → calls `publishEvent()` directly, skipping D.1 through D.5.\n\n**When both D.5 and D.6 fail in sequence:** `syncAttemptCount` is incremented twice (once by D.5 failure path, once by D.6). This is correct — two distinct failures occurred.\n\n---\n\n### 10. Slot Update EB Failure (207 Multi-Status)\n\n**Path:** `ShowSlotService.updateSlot()` → `EbEventSyncService.updateEvent()` or `EbCapacityService.updateCapacityTier()` fails after the internal DB update was already committed.\n\n**Rule:** The internal DB update is NOT rolled back. Internal DB is source of truth (PRODUCT.md §5.1).\n\n**Response: `207 Multi-Status`**\n```json\n{\n  \"internalUpdate\": \"SUCCESS\",\n  \"ebSyncStatus\": \"FAILED\",\n  \"slotId\": 101,\n  \"error\": \"Eventbrite update failed: connection timeout. Internal record updated successfully.\",\n  \"lastSyncError\": \"EB update failed: connect timeout after 10s\"\n}\n```\n\n**State effect:** Slot remains `ACTIVE`. `lastSyncError` is recorded. `syncAttemptCount` is NOT incremented (this is a post-ACTIVE update failure, not a sync failure on the way to ACTIVE).\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 39
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:50:56.406Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:50:56.406Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_vN2vcvjKSkusBiWdFbmd1c3G",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 7207fd\nWall time: 0.0512 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1166\nOutput:\n# TEST_AGENT.md — Testing Standards & Execution Agent\n\n> Read this file before writing or running tests.\n> This agent is responsible for test planning, test implementation, execution, failure fixing, and result logging.\n\n---\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis file defines the responsibilities of the **Test Agent**.\n\n- Build tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8 test list (source of truth for test scope)\n- Follow project testing architecture in `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n- Execute tests and fix failures caused by new or changed code\n- Keep production code and test code aligned with module boundaries from `PRODUCT.md`\n- Produce a timestamped test result log in `test_result_folder/` at the repo root\n\n---\n\n## Scope Ownership\n\n### Test Agent MUST\n\n- Write tests for all layers listed in spec:\n  - `domain/` (pure unit)\n  - `service/` (Mockito orchestration)\n  - `api/` (`@WebMvcTest` HTTP contract)\n  - `arch/` (ArchUnit boundaries)\n- Run relevant tests after implementation\n- Fix failing tests and related test setup issues\n- Fix production code only when required to satisfy approved spec behavior\n- Log all found errors and all applied fixes in a test report file\n\n### Test Agent MUST NOT\n\n- Introduce unapproved features not present in `SPEC_*.md`\n- Change module ownership or dependency direction rules\n- Add module-level `@ControllerAdvice`\n- Skip failed tests without documenting reason and impact\n\n---\n\n## Inputs (Required Before Writing Tests)\n\n1. Confirmed feature spec: `SPEC_*.md`\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Parse Stage 8 test list from the approved `SPEC_*.md`\n2. Generate/adjust tests by layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All feasible Stage 8 tests are implemented\n- Relevant tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` validates behavior, fixes test/logic defects, and publishes the final test report\n"
  }
}
```

Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All feasible Stage 8 tests are implemented\n- Relevant tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` validates behavior, fixes test/logic defects, and publishes the final test report\n"

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:50:58.292Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UHEWcrGW9h5OrE6eSew6SN0m",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 02f547\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1632\nOutput:\n# Testing Guide — Modular Monolith\n\n> Tests are not optional. Every feature must have coverage across all four layers.\n> The `arch/` tests in particular are self-enforcing — they fail the build automatically\n> if anyone violates a module boundary. Never delete or weaken an arch test.\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle — Each Layer Tests One Thing\n\nThe most common testing mistake is putting the wrong test in the wrong layer.\nUse this rule: **each layer tests exactly one concern.**\n\n| Layer | Tests One Concern | Spring Context? | Key Annotation |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `domain/` | Business rules on the domain object | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n| `service/` | Orchestration — right calls in right order | None | `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` |\n| `api/` | HTTP contract — status codes, JSON shape, validation | Web layer only | `@WebMvcTest` |\n| `arch/` | Architectural boundaries | None | `@AnalyzeClasses` |\n| `mapper/` | Field translation correctness | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n\n---\n\n## Test Folder Structure\n\nEvery module must mirror its source structure exactly:\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/\n    ├── main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    │   ├── api/controller/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   ├── service/\n    │   ├── repository/\n    │   ├── mapper/\n    │   └── event/\n    └── test/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n        ├── domain/        ← Pure unit tests. No Spring. No infrastructure mocks.\n        ├── service/       ← Mockito only. Mocks repositories and ACL facades.\n        ├── api/           ← @WebMvcTest slice. Mocks service and mapper.\n        ├── arch/          ← ArchUnit. Enforces module boundary rules at build time.\n        └── mapper/        ← MapStruct translation tests. No Spring.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Test Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\nAll test methods must follow this exact pattern:\n\n```\nshould_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]\n```\n\nThe name must be readable as a sentence describing the behaviour being verified.\nNever use vague names like `testConfirm()`, `test1()`, or `happyPath()`.\n\n**Good examples:**\n```\nshould_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending\nshould_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking\nshould_return_200_when_booking_is_found\nshould_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum\nshould_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist\nshould_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_after_successful_confirmation\nshould_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_id_is_unknown\nshould_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_status\n```\n\n**Bad examples (never use):**\n```\ntestConfirm()\ntest_happy_path()\nconfirmWorks()\nbooking_test_1()\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 1 — `domain/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the domain object enforces its own rules correctly.\nThese are the fastest tests in the suite — they must run in milliseconds.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- Valid state transitions succeed and produce the correct new state\n- Invalid state transitions throw `BusinessRuleException` with the correct message\n- Guard conditions (preconditions on methods) are enforced\n- Value object construction validates its invariants\n- Terminal states cannot be transitioned out of\n- Null or invalid inputs are rejected\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Persistence (no repositories, no DB)\n- HTTP (no controllers, no MockMvc)\n- Spring beans (no `@SpringBootTest`, no `@ExtendWith(SpringExtension.class)`)\n- Mocks of infrastructure — if you need a mock here, the domain has an infrastructure\n  dependency. Fix the domain, not the test.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Plain JUnit 5 only.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — no annotations on the class\nclass BookingTest {\n    ...\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — domain tests never load Spring\n@SpringBootTest\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n\n// ❌ Wrong — if you need this, your domain imports infrastructure\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest that the domain method succeeds and produces the correct outcome under valid conditions.\nCover every valid state transition defined in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Happy path: valid transition succeeds\n    @Test\n    void should_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        // Arrange — set up the entity in a valid starting state\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        // Act — call the domain method\n        booking.confirm();\n\n        // Assert — verify the expected outcome\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_confirmed_and_event_is_more_than_48h_away() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusDays(3);\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\nTest that invalid operations are rejected with the correct exception and message.\nCover every forbidden transition in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Negative path: invalid transition throws BusinessRuleException\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_an_already_confirmed_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Booking is already confirmed\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_cancelling_within_48h_of_event() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusHours(24); // inside the 48h window\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n"
  }
}

## Entry 45
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:51:00.154Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:00.154Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_3QPbXSawHDO5BsG26unjXzPo",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ff13ff\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1746\nOutput:\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Exception / Edge Case Tests\n\nTest boundary conditions, null inputs, minimum/maximum values, and terminal states.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_booking_id_is_null() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(null, BookingStatus.PENDING))\n            .isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING, 9))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot book more than 8 seats\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_operating_on_a_terminal_cancelled_state() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        // All operations on a terminal state must be rejected\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 2 — `service/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the service correctly orchestrates domain objects, repositories, ACL facades,\nand event publishing. These tests prove the *use case* works — not the domain rule, not the\nHTTP contract.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- The correct repository method is called with the correct arguments\n- The correct ACL facade method is called when an external system is involved\n- `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` is called with the correct event type and payload\n- `BookingNotFoundException` (or equivalent) is thrown when the entity is not found\n- `BusinessRuleException` propagates correctly when the domain rejects an operation\n- `[System]IntegrationException` is thrown (and compensation is triggered) when an external call fails\n- No event is published when the use case fails\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Domain rules — those are tested in `domain/`. Do not re-test `booking.confirm()` logic here.\n- HTTP concerns — no MockMvc, no status codes.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) // Mockito only — no Spring\nclass BookingServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private BookingRepository bookingRepository;     // Always mock the repository\n\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher; // Always mock the event publisher\n\n    @Mock\n    private PaymentAclService paymentAclService;      // Mock ACL facades for external systems\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private BookingService bookingService;            // The class under test — real instance\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest the complete use case: entity is found, domain method succeeds, repository is saved,\nevent is published, ACL facade is called if required.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_confirm_booking_and_publish_event_when_booking_is_pending() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    // Assert — verify orchestration\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(booking);                               // saved\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingConfirmedEvent.class)); // event fired\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_initiate_booking_and_call_payment_facade_when_request_is_valid() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenAnswer(inv -> inv.getArgument(0));\n\n    // Act\n    Booking result = bookingService.initiateBooking(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    verify(paymentAclService).charge(any(ChargeRequest.class));           // external call made\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(any(Booking.class));                    // persisted\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingInitiatedEvent.class)); // event fired\n    assertThat(result.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\n#### Entity Not Found\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_does_not_exist() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId))\n        .isInstanceOf(BookingNotFoundException.class);\n\n    // Verify no side effects occurred\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n}\n```\n\n#### Domain Rule Violation Propagates\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_propagate_BusinessRuleException_when_domain_rejects_confirmation() {\n    // Arrange — entity is in an invalid state for this operation\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act + Assert — service must NOT swallow domain exceptions\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId))\n        .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n        .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n\n    // Verify no side effects — nothing saved, no event fired\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n}\n```\n\n#### External System Failure\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_throw_PaymentIntegrationException_when_payment_facade_fails() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any()))\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\"Payment gateway unavailable\"));\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.initiateBooking(request))\n        .isInstanceOf(PaymentIntegrationException.class);\n\n    // Verify no booking was persisted and no event was fired\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_refund_and_throw_when_payment_succeeds_but_db_write_fails() {\n    // Arrange — payment succeeds but save throws\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenThrow(new RuntimeException(\"DB unavailable\"));\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.initiateBooking(request))\n        .isInstanceOf(RuntimeException.class);\n\n"
  }
}
```

These tests prove the *use case* works — not the domain rule, not the\nHTTP contract.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- The correct repository method is called with the correct arguments\n- The correct ACL facade method is called when an external system is involved\n- `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` is called with the correct event type and payload\n- `BookingNotFoundException` (or equivalent) is thrown when the entity is not found\n- `BusinessRuleException` propagates correctly when the domain rejects an operation\n- `[System]IntegrationException` is thrown (and compensation is triggered) when an external call fails\n- No event is published when the use case fails\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Domain rules — those are tested in `domain/`. Do not re-test `booking.confirm()` logic here.\n- HTTP concerns — no MockMvc, no status codes.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) // Mockito only — no Spring\nclass BookingServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private BookingRepository bookingRepository;     // Always mock the repository\n\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher; // Always mock the event publisher\n\n    @Mock\n    private PaymentAclService paymentAclService;      // Mock ACL facades for external systems\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private BookingService bookingService;            // The class under test — real instance\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest the complete use case: entity is found, domain method succeeds, repository is saved,\nevent is published, ACL facade is called if required.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_confirm_booking_and_publish_event_when_booking_is_pending() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    // Assert — verify orchestration\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(booking);                               // saved\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingConfirmedEvent.class)); // event fired\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_initiate_booking_and_call_payment_facade_when_request_is_valid() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenAnswer(inv -> inv.getArgument(0));\n\n    // Act\n    Booking result = bookingService.initiateBooking(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    verify(paymentAclService).charge(any(ChargeRequest.class));           // external call made\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(any(Booking.class));                    // persisted\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingInitiatedEvent.class)); // event fired\n    assertThat(result.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\n#### Entity Not Found\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_does_not_exist() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId))\n        .isInstanceOf(BookingNotFoundException.class);\n\n    // Verify no side effects occurred\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n}\n```\n\n#### Domain Rule Violation Propagates\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_propagate_BusinessRuleException_when_domain_rejects_confirmation() {\n    // Arrange — entity is in an invalid state for this operation\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act + Assert — service must NOT swallow domain exceptions\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId))\n        .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n        .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n\n    // Verify no side effects — nothing saved, no event fired\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n}\n```\n\n#### External System Failure\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_throw_PaymentIntegrationException_when_payment_facade_fails() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any()))\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\"Payment gateway unavailable\"));\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.initiateBooking(request))\n        .isInstanceOf(PaymentIntegrationException.class);\n\n    // Verify no booking was persisted and no event was fired\n    verify(bookingRepository, never()).save(any());\n    verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any());\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_refund_and_throw_when_payment_succeeds_but_db_write_fails() {\n    // Arrange — payment succeeds but save throws\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenThrow(new RuntimeException(\"DB unavailable\"));\n\n    // Act + Assert\n    assertThatThrownBy(() -> bookingService.initiateBooking(request))\n        .isInstanceOf(RuntimeException.class);\n\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: d0deb2\nWall time: 0.0513 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3859\nOutput:\n\n    // Verify compensation — refund must be called\n    verify(paymentAclService).refund(paymentIntentId);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Event Payload Tests\n\nVerify that published events carry the correct data — the consumer depends on this contract.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_with_correct_booking_id_and_user_id() {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    ArgumentCaptor<BookingConfirmedEvent> captor =\n        ArgumentCaptor.forClass(BookingConfirmedEvent.class);\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(captor.capture());\n\n    BookingConfirmedEvent event = captor.getValue();\n    assertThat(event.bookingId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\n    assertThat(event.userId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    // Verify no @Entity objects leaked into the event\n    assertThat(event).isNotInstanceOf(Booking.class);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 3 — `api/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify the HTTP contract: correct status codes, correct response JSON shape, request\nvalidation enforcement, and `@PreAuthorize` role checks. Nothing else.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- `GET` returns 200 with correctly shaped JSON when found\n- `POST` returns 201 with correctly shaped JSON when created\n- Required fields missing → 400 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Field constraint violated (e.g. value out of range) → 400\n- Entity not found → 404 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Business rule violated → 422 with `ErrorResponse`\n- External system failure → 502 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Unauthenticated request to secured endpoint → 401\n- Insufficient role → 403\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\nBusiness logic. Mock the service to return a fixed value and test only the HTTP layer.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@WebMvcTest(BookingController.class)  // loads ONLY the web layer\nclass BookingControllerTest {\n\n    @Autowired\n    private MockMvc mockMvc;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private BookingService bookingService;   // Mock — do not test its logic here\n\n    @MockBean\n    private BookingMapper bookingMapper;     // Mock — do not test mapping here\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_200_and_booking_response_when_booking_is_found() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    BookingResponse response = new BookingResponse(bookingId, \"CONFIRMED\", userId);\n\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId)).thenReturn(mock(Booking.class));\n    when(bookingMapper.toResponse(any())).thenReturn(response);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isOk())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.id\").value(bookingId.toString()))\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"CONFIRMED\"));\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_201_and_booking_response_when_booking_is_created() throws Exception {\n    BookingResponse response = new BookingResponse(UUID.randomUUID(), \"PENDING\", userId);\n\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any())).thenReturn(mock(Booking.class));\n    when(bookingMapper.toResponse(any())).thenReturn(response);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(\"\"\"\n                {\n                  \"seatIds\": [\"seat-uuid-1\"],\n                  \"paymentMethodId\": \"pm_test_123\"\n                }\n                \"\"\")\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isCreated())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"PENDING\"));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Validation (400)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_ids_is_missing() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(\"{}\")   // missing required fields\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").exists());  // ErrorResponse shape\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() throws Exception {\n    // Build a request with 9 seats (max is 8)\n    String body = buildRequestWithSeatCount(9);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(body)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Not Found (404)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId))\n        .thenThrow(new BookingNotFoundException(bookingId));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isNotFound())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\n            containsString(\"Booking with id \" + bookingId + \" not found\")));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Business Rule Violation (422)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_422_when_cancellation_window_has_passed() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingService.cancelBooking(bookingId))\n        .thenThrow(new BusinessRuleException(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\"));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(delete(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isUnprocessableEntity())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\n            containsString(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours\")));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — External System Failure (502)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_502_when_payment_gateway_is_unavailable() throws Exception {\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any()))\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\"Payment gateway unavailable\"));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(validRequestBody())\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadGateway())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\"A payment error occurred. Please try again.\"));\n    // Verify: internal error message is NOT exposed to the client\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Authorization Tests\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_401_when_request_is_unauthenticated() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(validRequestBody()))\n        // No Authorization header\n        .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized());\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_403_when_user_lacks_admin_role() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/admin/bookings\")\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer user-token\")) // ROLE_USER, not ROLE_ADMIN\n        .andExpect(status().isForbidden());\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 4 — `arch/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nEnforce architectural boundaries at build time using ArchUnit. A failing arch test means\nsomeone violated a boundary — **fix the code, never weaken the rule.**\n\nEvery module must have exactly one `[Module]ArchTest.java` in its `arch/` test folder.\nThese run as part of the normal test suite — no special setup or CI configuration needed.\n\n### Mandatory Rules (Copy for Every Module)\n\n```java\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.[org].[module]\")\nclass [Module]ArchTest {\n\n    // Rule 1: Domain must be pure — no infrastructure dependencies\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule domain_must_not_depend_on_infrastructure =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].domain..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[module].repository..\",\n                \"org.springframework.web..\",\n                \"jakarta.persistence..\",\n                \"org.springframework.data..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"Domain must be pure Java — no JPA, no HTTP, no Spring annotations\");\n\n    // Rule 2: Controllers must not bypass the service layer\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule controllers_must_not_import_repositories =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].api.controller..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].repository..\")\n            .because(\"Controllers must delegate to service — never access repositories directly\");\n\n    // Rule 3: No module imports another module's repository\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_repository_access =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[other-module-a].repository..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].repository..\"\n                // add all peer and downstream modules here\n            )\n            .because(\"Modules must never import another module's repository — call their API instead\");\n\n    // Rule 4: Events must carry only primitives and value objects — never JPA entities\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule events_must_not_contain_entities =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].event.published..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().areAnnotatedWith(Entity.class)\n            .because(\"Events must carry only primitives, IDs, enums, or value objects — never JPA entities\");\n\n    // Rule 5: Mappers must not call services — pure translation only\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule mappers_must_not_call_services =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].mapper..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].service..\")\n            .because(\"Mappers are pure translation — they must not call services or trigger business logic\");\n\n    // Rule 6: No direct external system calls — must use shared/ ACL facades\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_eventbrite_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.eventbrite.client..\")\n            .because(\"Eventbrite calls must use EbEventSyncService, EbTicketService, etc. from shared/eventbrite/service/\");\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_razorpay_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.razorpay..\")\n            .because(\"Razorpay calls must use RazorpayAclService from shared/payment/service/\");\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_openai_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.openai..\")\n            .because(\"OpenAI calls must use ChatbotAclService from shared/ai/service/\");\n\n    // Rule 7: No module imports another module's service, entity, or repository\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_service_imports =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[other-module-a].service..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].service..\",\n                \"..[other-module-a].domain..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].domain..\",\n                \"..[other-module-a].repository..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].repository..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"Modules communicate only via REST API or Spring Events — never import another module's internal classes\");\n\n    // Rule 8: shared/ can NEVER depend on any module\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule shared_must_not_depend_on_modules =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..shared..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..discovery-catalog..\",\n                \"..scheduling..\",\n                \"..identity..\",\n                \"..booking-inventory..\",\n                \"..payments-ticketing..\",\n                \"..promotions..\",\n                \"..engagement..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"shared/ is foundational — it cannot depend on any module\");\n\n    // Rule 9: Only one GlobalExceptionHandler exists in shared/common/exception/\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule only_one_global_exception_handler =\n        noClasses()\n            .that()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].api.exception..\")\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class)\n            .because(\"All exception handling routes through shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler — never add @ControllerAdvice in a module\");\n\n    // Rule 10: Dependency direction is strictly downward; no circular imports\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule module_dependency_direction_is_downward =\n        layeredArchitecture()\n            .layer(\"shared\").definedBy(\"..shared..\")\n            .layer(\"discovery-catalog\").definedBy(\"..discovery-catalog..\")\n            .layer(\"scheduling\").definedBy(\"..scheduling..\")\n            .layer(\"identity\").definedBy(\"..identity..\")\n            .layer(\"booking-inventory\").definedBy(\"..booking-inventory..\")\n            .layer(\"payments-ticketing\").definedBy(\"..payments-ticketing..\")\n            .layer(\"promotions\").definedBy(\"..promotions..\")\n            .layer(\"engagement\").definedBy(\"..engagement..\")\n            .layer(\"admin\").definedBy(\"..admin..\")\n            .layer(\"app\").definedBy(\"..app..\")\n            \n            .whereLayer(\"app\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            .whereLayer(\"admin\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            .whereLayer(\"engagement\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"promotions\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"payments-ticketing\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"booking-inventory\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"scheduling\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"identity\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"discovery-catalog\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"scheduling\", \"identity\", \"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"shared\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            \n            .because(\"Dependency order is: shared ← discovery-catalog ← scheduling/identity ← booking-inventory ← payments-ticketing ← promotions ← engagement ← admin ← app. No circular dependencies allowed.\");\n}\n```\n\n### Testing Eventbrite Integration Rules\n\nAdd these additional rules to any module that integrates with Eventbrite to enforce ACL facade usage:\n\n```java\n    // Rule: Only EbEventSyncService may read Eventbrite events\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: d0deb2\nWall time: 0.0513 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3859\nOutput:\n\n    // Verify compensation — refund must be called\n    verify(paymentAclService).refund(paymentIntentId);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Event Payload Tests\n\nVerify that published events carry the correct data — the consumer depends on this contract.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_with_correct_booking_id_and_user_id() {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    ArgumentCaptor<BookingConfirmedEvent> captor =\n        ArgumentCaptor.forClass(BookingConfirmedEvent.class);\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(captor.capture());\n\n    BookingConfirmedEvent event = captor.getValue();\n    assertThat(event.bookingId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\n    assertThat(event.userId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    // Verify no @Entity objects leaked into the event\n    assertThat(event).isNotInstanceOf(Booking.class);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 3 — `api/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify the HTTP contract: correct status codes, correct response JSON shape, request\nvalidation enforcement, and `@PreAuthorize` role checks. Nothing else.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- `GET` returns 200 with correctly shaped JSON when found\n- `POST` returns 201 with correctly shaped JSON when created\n- Required fields missing → 400 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Field constraint violated (e.g. value out of range) → 400\n- Entity not found → 404 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Business rule violated → 422 with `ErrorResponse`\n- External system failure → 502 with `ErrorResponse`\n- Unauthenticated request to secured endpoint → 401\n- Insufficient role → 403\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\nBusiness logic. Mock the service to return a fixed value and test only the HTTP layer.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@WebMvcTest(BookingController.class)  // loads ONLY the web layer\nclass BookingControllerTest {\n\n    @Autowired\n    private MockMvc mockMvc;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private BookingService bookingService;   // Mock — do not test its logic here\n\n    @MockBean\n    private BookingMapper bookingMapper;     // Mock — do not test mapping here\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_200_and_booking_response_when_booking_is_found() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    BookingResponse response = new BookingResponse(bookingId, \"CONFIRMED\", userId);\n\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId)).thenReturn(mock(Booking.class));\n    when(bookingMapper.toResponse(any())).thenReturn(response);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isOk())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.id\").value(bookingId.toString()))\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"CONFIRMED\"));\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_201_and_booking_response_when_booking_is_created() throws Exception {\n    BookingResponse response = new BookingResponse(UUID.randomUUID(), \"PENDING\", userId);\n\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any())).thenReturn(mock(Booking.class));\n    when(bookingMapper.toResponse(any())).thenReturn(response);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(\"\"\"\n                {\n                  \"seatIds\": [\"seat-uuid-1\"],\n                  \"paymentMethodId\": \"pm_test_123\"\n                }\n                \"\"\")\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isCreated())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"PENDING\"));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Validation (400)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_ids_is_missing() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(\"{}\")   // missing required fields\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").exists());  // ErrorResponse shape\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() throws Exception {\n    // Build a request with 9 seats (max is 8)\n    String body = buildRequestWithSeatCount(9);\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(body)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Not Found (404)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId))\n        .thenThrow(new BookingNotFoundException(bookingId));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isNotFound())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\n            containsString(\"Booking with id \" + bookingId + \" not found\")));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — Business Rule Violation (422)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_422_when_cancellation_window_has_passed() throws Exception {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    when(bookingService.cancelBooking(bookingId))\n        .thenThrow(new BusinessRuleException(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\"));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(delete(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", bookingId)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isUnprocessableEntity())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\n            containsString(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours\")));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests — External System Failure (502)\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_502_when_payment_gateway_is_unavailable() throws Exception {\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any()))\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\"Payment gateway unavailable\"));\n\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(validRequestBody())\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-token\"))\n        .andExpect(status().isBadGateway())\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.message\").value(\"A payment error occurred. Please try again.\"));\n    // Verify: internal error message is NOT exposed to the client\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Authorization Tests\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_401_when_request_is_unauthenticated() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/bookings\")\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\n            .content(validRequestBody()))\n        // No Authorization header\n        .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized());\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_return_403_when_user_lacks_admin_role() throws Exception {\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/admin/bookings\")\n            .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer user-token\")) // ROLE_USER, not ROLE_ADMIN\n        .andExpect(status().isForbidden());\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 4 — `arch/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nEnforce architectural boundaries at build time using ArchUnit. A failing arch test means\nsomeone violated a boundary — **fix the code, never weaken the rule.**\n\nEvery module must have exactly one `[Module]ArchTest.java` in its `arch/` test folder.\nThese run as part of the normal test suite — no special setup or CI configuration needed.\n\n### Mandatory Rules (Copy for Every Module)\n\n```java\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.[org].[module]\")\nclass [Module]ArchTest {\n\n    // Rule 1: Domain must be pure — no infrastructure dependencies\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule domain_must_not_depend_on_infrastructure =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].domain..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[module].repository..\",\n                \"org.springframework.web..\",\n                \"jakarta.persistence..\",\n                \"org.springframework.data..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"Domain must be pure Java — no JPA, no HTTP, no Spring annotations\");\n\n    // Rule 2: Controllers must not bypass the service layer\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule controllers_must_not_import_repositories =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].api.controller..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].repository..\")\n            .because(\"Controllers must delegate to service — never access repositories directly\");\n\n    // Rule 3: No module imports another module's repository\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_repository_access =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[other-module-a].repository..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].repository..\"\n                // add all peer and downstream modules here\n            )\n            .because(\"Modules must never import another module's repository — call their API instead\");\n\n    // Rule 4: Events must carry only primitives and value objects — never JPA entities\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule events_must_not_contain_entities =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].event.published..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().areAnnotatedWith(Entity.class)\n            .because(\"Events must carry only primitives, IDs, enums, or value objects — never JPA entities\");\n\n    // Rule 5: Mappers must not call services — pure translation only\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule mappers_must_not_call_services =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].mapper..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].service..\")\n            .because(\"Mappers are pure translation — they must not call services or trigger business logic\");\n\n    // Rule 6: No direct external system calls — must use shared/ ACL facades\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_eventbrite_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.eventbrite.client..\")\n            .because(\"Eventbrite calls must use EbEventSyncService, EbTicketService, etc. from shared/eventbrite/service/\");\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_razorpay_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.razorpay..\")\n            .because(\"Razorpay calls must use RazorpayAclService from shared/payment/service/\");\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_openai_calls =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"com.openai..\")\n            .because(\"OpenAI calls must use ChatbotAclService from shared/ai/service/\");\n\n    // Rule 7: No module imports another module's service, entity, or repository\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_service_imports =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..[other-module-a].service..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].service..\",\n                \"..[other-module-a].domain..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].domain..\",\n                \"..[other-module-a].repository..\",\n                \"..[other-module-b].repository..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"Modules communicate only via REST API or Spring Events — never import another module's internal classes\");\n\n    // Rule 8: shared/ can NEVER depend on any module\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule shared_must_not_depend_on_modules =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..shared..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..discovery-catalog..\",\n                \"..scheduling..\",\n                \"..identity..\",\n                \"..booking-inventory..\",\n                \"..payments-ticketing..\",\n                \"..promotions..\",\n                \"..engagement..\"\n            )\n            .because(\"shared/ is foundational — it cannot depend on any module\");\n\n    // Rule 9: Only one GlobalExceptionHandler exists in shared/common/exception/\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule only_one_global_exception_handler =\n        noClasses()\n            .that()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..[module].api.exception..\")\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class)\n            .because(\"All exception handling routes through shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler — never add @ControllerAdvice in a module\");\n\n    // Rule 10: Dependency direction is strictly downward; no circular imports\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule module_dependency_direction_is_downward =\n        layeredArchitecture()\n            .layer(\"shared\").definedBy(\"..shared..\")\n            .layer(\"discovery-catalog\").definedBy(\"..discovery-catalog..\")\n            .layer(\"scheduling\").definedBy(\"..scheduling..\")\n            .layer(\"identity\").definedBy(\"..identity..\")\n            .layer(\"booking-inventory\").definedBy(\"..booking-inventory..\")\n            .layer(\"payments-ticketing\").definedBy(\"..payments-ticketing..\")\n            .layer(\"promotions\").definedBy(\"..promotions..\")\n            .layer(\"engagement\").definedBy(\"..engagement..\")\n            .layer(\"admin\").definedBy(\"..admin..\")\n            .layer(\"app\").definedBy(\"..app..\")\n            \n            .whereLayer(\"app\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            .whereLayer(\"admin\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            .whereLayer(\"engagement\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"promotions\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"payments-ticketing\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"booking-inventory\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"scheduling\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"identity\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"discovery-catalog\").mayNotAccessLayers(\"scheduling\", \"identity\", \"booking-inventory\", \"payments-ticketing\", \"promotions\", \"engagement\", \"admin\", \"app\")\n            .whereLayer(\"shared\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\n            \n            .because(\"Dependency order is: shared ← discovery-catalog ← scheduling/identity ← booking-inventory ← payments-ticketing ← promotions ← engagement ← admin ← app. No circular dependencies allowed.\");\n}\n```\n\n### Testing Eventbrite Integration Rules\n\nAdd these additional rules to any module that integrates with Eventbrite to enforce ACL facade usage:\n\n```java\n    // Rule: Only EbEventSyncService may read Eventbrite events\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\n"
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:06.137Z",
