  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_FxXmbKN1761eetB4Tr3V0HmX",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 8f814c\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1847\nOutput:\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbEventSyncService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbVenueService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbScheduleService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbTicketService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbCapacityService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbOrderService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbAttendeeService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbDiscountSyncService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbRefundService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbWebhookService\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..eventbrite..\")\n            .because(\"Only the 10 ACL facades in shared/eventbrite/service/ may call Eventbrite API\");\n\n    // Rule: User and Order creation must never happen via Eventbrite API\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_eventbrite_user_or_order_creation =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].service..\")\n            .should().callMethodWhere(\n                target -> target.getFullName().contains(\"EbAttendeeService.createUser\") ||\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\"EbOrderService.createOrder\") ||\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\"EbOrderService.create\")\n            )\n            .because(\"User registration and order creation are internal-only — Eventbrite has no API for these; \" +\n                     \"use identity module for users and booking module for orders; JavaScript SDK handles Eventbrite order creation\");\n```\n\n### When to Add a New Arch Rule\n\nAdd a new rule to `[Module]ArchTest.java` whenever:\n- A new architectural decision is made for this module\n- A new layer or package is introduced\n- A new external system integration is added\n- A code review reveals a violation that should be prevented automatically in future\n\nClear example: If you implement a seat-locking state machine, add a rule preventing any @Service from bypassing the state machine and directly updating seat status.\n\nNever add a rule to suppress a valid violation. Fix the code.\n\n---\n\n## Mapper Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that every field is correctly translated in both directions: domain → response DTO,\nand request DTO → domain object. Mappers are the contract between your domain and your API —\na wrong mapping silently breaks both.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Instantiate the MapStruct implementation directly.\n\n```java\nclass BookingMapperTest {\n    // Instantiate directly — no Spring context needed\n    private final BookingMapper mapper = Mappers.getMapper(BookingMapper.class);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path — Domain to Response\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_fields() {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\n\n    assertThat(response.getId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\n    assertThat(response.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    assertThat(response.getStatus()).isEqualTo(\"CONFIRMED\");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path — Request DTO to Domain\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_create_request_to_booking_domain_with_correct_fields() {\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    List<UUID> seatIds = List.of(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID());\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\n\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\n\n    assertThat(booking.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    assertThat(booking.getSeatIds()).containsExactlyElementsOf(seatIds);\n    assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Edge Case — Null / Optional Fields\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_null_cancellation_reason_to_null_in_response() {\n    Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    booking.setCancellationReason(null);\n\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\n\n    assertThat(response.getCancellationReason()).isNull();\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_map_optional_notes_field_when_present() {\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\n    request.setNotes(\"Wheelchair access required\");\n\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\n\n    assertThat(booking.getNotes()).isEqualTo(\"Wheelchair access required\");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference — Which Test Goes Where?\n\nUse this table to decide the correct layer for any test scenario:\n\n| Scenario | Correct Layer | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `booking.confirm()` succeeds when status is PENDING | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\n| `booking.confirm()` throws when status is CANCELLED | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` saves and publishes event | `service/` | Orchestration |\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` throws when booking not found | `service/` | Orchestration — entity lookup |\n| `BookingService.initiateBooking()` calls PaymentAclService | `service/` | Orchestration — ACL call |\n| `BookingService` refunds when DB write fails after payment | `service/` | Compensation logic |\n| `GET /api/bookings/{id}` returns 200 with correct JSON | `api/` | HTTP contract |\n| `POST /api/bookings` returns 400 for missing seatIds | `api/` | Request validation |\n| `DELETE /api/bookings/{id}` returns 403 without admin role | `api/` | Authorization |\n| No class in `booking.domain` imports a `@Repository` | `arch/` | Boundary rule |\n| No class in `booking` imports `payments.repository` | `arch/` | Cross-module boundary |\n| Events in `booking.event.published` carry no `@Entity` | `arch/` | Event payload rule |\n| `BookingMapper.toResponse()` maps all fields correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\n| `BookingMapper.toDomain()` maps request to domain correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\n\n---\n\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\n\n| Mistake | Why It's Wrong | Fix |\n|---|---|---|\n| Testing `booking.confirm()` logic in `service/` tests | Re-tests domain rules — adds no value | Move to `domain/` |\n| Using `@SpringBootTest` in `service/` or `domain/` tests | Loads entire context — massively slow | Use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` or no annotation |\n| Putting `@MockBean` in `domain/` tests | If you need a mock, domain has an infra dependency | Remove the dependency from the domain class |\n| Testing business logic in `api/` tests | Controller tests should only care about HTTP contract | Mock the service; move logic test to `service/` or `domain/` |\n| Deleting or weakening an `arch/` rule to make a test pass | Hides a real architectural violation | Fix the production code, not the test |\n| Using `assertThat(response).isNotNull()` as the only assertion | Proves nothing — a null check is not a contract | Assert every field that matters to the caller |\n| Missing event payload assertions in `service/` tests | A wrong payload breaks the consuming module silently | Use `ArgumentCaptor` to assert every event field |\n| Mapping fields inside `service/` or `controller/` tests | Indicates production code is mapping in the wrong place | All mapping belongs in `mapper/` — fix the source |\n"
  }
}

    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_FxXmbKN1761eetB4Tr3V0HmX",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 8f814c\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1847\nOutput:\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module]..\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbEventSyncService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbVenueService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbScheduleService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbTicketService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbCapacityService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbOrderService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbAttendeeService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbDiscountSyncService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbRefundService\")\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\"EbWebhookService\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAPackage(\"..eventbrite..\")\n            .because(\"Only the 10 ACL facades in shared/eventbrite/service/ may call Eventbrite API\");\n\n    // Rule: User and Order creation must never happen via Eventbrite API\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule no_eventbrite_user_or_order_creation =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..[module].service..\")\n            .should().callMethodWhere(\n                target -> target.getFullName().contains(\"EbAttendeeService.createUser\") ||\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\"EbOrderService.createOrder\") ||\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\"EbOrderService.create\")\n            )\n            .because(\"User registration and order creation are internal-only — Eventbrite has no API for these; \" +\n                     \"use identity module for users and booking module for orders; JavaScript SDK handles Eventbrite order creation\");\n```\n\n### When to Add a New Arch Rule\n\nAdd a new rule to `[Module]ArchTest.java` whenever:\n- A new architectural decision is made for this module\n- A new layer or package is introduced\n- A new external system integration is added\n- A code review reveals a violation that should be prevented automatically in future\n\nClear example: If you implement a seat-locking state machine, add a rule preventing any @Service from bypassing the state machine and directly updating seat status.\n\nNever add a rule to suppress a valid violation. Fix the code.\n\n---\n\n## Mapper Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that every field is correctly translated in both directions: domain → response DTO,\nand request DTO → domain object. Mappers are the contract between your domain and your API —\na wrong mapping silently breaks both.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Instantiate the MapStruct implementation directly.\n\n```java\nclass BookingMapperTest {\n    // Instantiate directly — no Spring context needed\n    private final BookingMapper mapper = Mappers.getMapper(BookingMapper.class);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path — Domain to Response\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_fields() {\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\n\n    assertThat(response.getId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\n    assertThat(response.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    assertThat(response.getStatus()).isEqualTo(\"CONFIRMED\");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path — Request DTO to Domain\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_create_request_to_booking_domain_with_correct_fields() {\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    List<UUID> seatIds = List.of(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID());\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\n\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\n\n    assertThat(booking.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\n    assertThat(booking.getSeatIds()).containsExactlyElementsOf(seatIds);\n    assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Edge Case — Null / Optional Fields\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_map_null_cancellation_reason_to_null_in_response() {\n    Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    booking.setCancellationReason(null);\n\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\n\n    assertThat(response.getCancellationReason()).isNull();\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_map_optional_notes_field_when_present() {\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\n    request.setNotes(\"Wheelchair access required\");\n\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\n\n    assertThat(booking.getNotes()).isEqualTo(\"Wheelchair access required\");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference — Which Test Goes Where?\n\nUse this table to decide the correct layer for any test scenario:\n\n| Scenario | Correct Layer | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `booking.confirm()` succeeds when status is PENDING | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\n| `booking.confirm()` throws when status is CANCELLED | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` saves and publishes event | `service/` | Orchestration |\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` throws when booking not found | `service/` | Orchestration — entity lookup |\n| `BookingService.initiateBooking()` calls PaymentAclService | `service/` | Orchestration — ACL call |\n| `BookingService` refunds when DB write fails after payment | `service/` | Compensation logic |\n| `GET /api/bookings/{id}` returns 200 with correct JSON | `api/` | HTTP contract |\n| `POST /api/bookings` returns 400 for missing seatIds | `api/` | Request validation |\n| `DELETE /api/bookings/{id}` returns 403 without admin role | `api/` | Authorization |\n| No class in `booking.domain` imports a `@Repository` | `arch/` | Boundary rule |\n| No class in `booking` imports `payments.repository` | `arch/` | Cross-module boundary |\n| Events in `booking.event.published` carry no `@Entity` | `arch/` | Event payload rule |\n| `BookingMapper.toResponse()` maps all fields correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\n| `BookingMapper.toDomain()` maps request to domain correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\n\n---\n\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\n\n| Mistake | Why It's Wrong | Fix |\n|---|---|---|\n| Testing `booking.confirm()` logic in `service/` tests | Re-tests domain rules — adds no value | Move to `domain/` |\n| Using `@SpringBootTest` in `service/` or `domain/` tests | Loads entire context — massively slow | Use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` or no annotation |\n| Putting `@MockBean` in `domain/` tests | If you need a mock, domain has an infra dependency | Remove the dependency from the domain class |\n| Testing business logic in `api/` tests | Controller tests should only care about HTTP contract | Mock the service; move logic test to `service/` or `domain/` |\n| Deleting or weakening an `arch/` rule to make a test pass | Hides a real architectural violation | Fix the production code, not the test |\n| Using `assertThat(response).isNotNull()` as the only assertion | Proves nothing — a null check is not a contract | Assert every field that matters to the caller |\n| Missing event payload assertions in `service/` tests | A wrong payload breaks the consuming module silently | Use `ArgumentCaptor` to assert every event field |\n| Mapping fields inside `service/` or `controller/` tests | Indicates production code is mapping in the wrong place | All mapping belongs in `mapper/` — fix the source |\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 62
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:51:41.607Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:41.607Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb353-38a5-7d93-87d8-527755cf467e",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 69
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:51:48.069Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:48.069Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_yt4sRX4D3P0uGsavbLnfnfp8",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 02fccc\nWall time: 0.0508 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2692\nOutput:\n# PRODUCT.md — Module Registry & Product Map\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026\n\n**Architecture:** Modular Monolith — Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21\n\n**External Integrations:** Eventbrite (ticketing + payments + orders) | OpenAI GPT-4o (AI assistant)\n\n⚠ **Important:** This is the single source of truth for module ownership, boundaries, inter-module communication, and the Eventbrite ACL. Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads event/venue via REST)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org. **NO order creation via API** — orders created exclusively by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. **NO attendee creation via API** — attendees created by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Read refund request status only from order `refund_request` field. **NO refund submission API.**\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` does NOT create orders. Orders are created exclusively by the Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK). `EbOrderService` only reads orders after the widget fires the `onOrderComplete` callback.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` does NOT create attendees. Attendees are created automatically when the Checkout Widget completes an order. `EbAttendeeService` only reads attendee records.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` does NOT submit refunds programmatically. Refund status is read from the order's `refund_request` field only. Actual refund initiation is handled by mimicking admin actions via Eventbrite org token (backend only).\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). For integration tests, add SQLite or PostgreSQL backend to the mock service.\n\n**Endpoints Mocked:**\n- `POST/GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` \n- `POST/GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/`\n- `POST/GET /ticket_classes/`\n- `POST/GET /inventory_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /capacity_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /orders/`\n- `POST/GET /attendees/`\n- `POST/GET /discounts/`\n- `POST/GET /webhooks/` (webhook registration)\n- ... and 100+ supporting endpoints\n\n**Configuration:**\n- **Spring Profile:** `mock` (Spring Boot automatically routes all ACL façade calls to mock service)\n- **Environment Variable:** `EVENTBRITE_API_URL=http://localhost:9000/v3/` (when using mock profile)\n- **Startup Command:** \n  ```bash\n  cd mock-eventbrite-api\n  python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000 --reload\n  ```\n\n**Trade-Offs:**\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| **Data Persistence** | In-memory; lost on shutdown. Acceptable for rapid iteration. |\n| **Latency** | Minimal (<5ms); no real network calls. |\n| **API Fidelity** | Matches Eventbrite API v3 schema exactly; test against real contract. |\n| **Concurrency** | Handles parallel requests; respects single-flight locks for testing. |\n| **Webhooks** | Simulates webhook delivery for testing event-driven flows. |\n\n**Usage Pattern:**\n- **Local Dev:** Run mock service; set `spring.profiles.active=mock` in `application-mock.properties`\n- **Integration Tests:** Use Spring `@ActiveProfiles(\"mock\")` to inject mock implementations\n- **Production:** Set `spring.profiles.active=production`; real Eventbrite API calls occur\n\n**See** `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` **for detailed mock service documentation, sample payloads, and configuration options.**\n\n## 5. Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. Eventbrite is the ticketing/payment backbone. Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge between both systems.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_order_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs a valid `event_id` to render\n- FR6 (Cancellation) — Eventbrite cancel API needs a valid event tied to a valid order\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking requires a confirmed Eventbrite order under that `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks. No tickets can be sold, no orders created, no refunds processed.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n"
  }
}
```

Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads event/venue via REST)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org. **NO order creation via API** — orders created exclusively by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. **NO attendee creation via API** — attendees created by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Read refund request status only from order `refund_request` field. **NO refund submission API.**\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` does NOT create orders. Orders are created exclusively by the Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK). `EbOrderService` only reads orders after the widget fires the `onOrderComplete` callback.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` does NOT create attendees. Attendees are created automatically when the Checkout Widget completes an order. `EbAttendeeService` only reads attendee records.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` does NOT submit refunds programmatically. Refund status is read from the order's `refund_request` field only. Actual refund initiation is handled by mimicking admin actions via Eventbrite org token (backend only).\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). {
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:50.229Z",

    "output": "Chunk ID: 9c8fd6\nWall time: 0.0508 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2836\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:51:53.533Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_enT71MjjfKTqAQVo2H4mEOh8",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6ae766\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1834\nOutput:\n# CODING_AGENT.md — Spring & Java Coding Standards\n\n> Read this file before writing any production code.\n> These are the coding standards for this codebase. Every class, method, and annotation\n> must follow these rules. When in doubt, refer to `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` for\n> where code lives and this file for how to write it.\n\n---\n\n## Before Writing Any Code — Checklist\n\n- [ ] Read `PRODUCT.md` — confirm which module owns this feature, check the inter-module event map and ACL facade names\n- [ ] Read the confirmed `SPEC.md` for this feature — domain model, API, business logic, error handling\n- [ ] Read `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` — confirm which package and layer each class belongs to\n- [ ] Read `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` — testing implementation/execution/logging ownership is delegated to the Test Agent\n\n### Scope Boundary for CODING_AGENT\n\n- CODING_AGENT owns **production code quality and architecture compliance**.\n- CODING_AGENT does **not** own end-to-end test authoring/execution workflow by default.\n- Test creation, execution, and failure/fix logging are owned by `agents/TEST_AGENT.md`.\n- If explicitly asked to write tests in the same task, follow `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` and coordinate with TEST_AGENT standards.\n\n---\n\n## Java & Spring Version Baseline\n\n```\nJava:          21 (use records, sealed classes, pattern matching, text blocks where appropriate)\nSpring Boot:   3.5.11\nSpring:        6.x\nMapStruct:     1.5.x\nLombok:        use sparingly — prefer Java records for DTOs and value objects\nJPA:           Jakarta Persistence (jakarta.persistence.*) — NOT javax.persistence\nFlyway:        version managed in parent pom.xml only\n```\n\n---\n\n## General Coding Rules\n\n### Immutability First\n\nPrefer immutable objects. Use Java records for DTOs, events, and value objects.\nOnly use mutable classes when the lifecycle explicitly requires mutation (e.g. JPA entities).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — record for response DTO\npublic record BookingResponse(UUID id, String status, UUID userId) {}\n\n// ✅ Correct — record for event payload\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(UUID bookingId, UUID userId, UUID showSlotId) {}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — mutable class for a DTO\npublic class BookingResponse {\n    private UUID id;\n    public void setId(UUID id) { this.id = id; }\n}\n```\n\n### No Nulls in Public APIs\n\nUse `Optional<T>` for values that may be absent. Never return `null` from a public method.\nNever accept `null` as a valid input — validate at the boundary with `@NotNull`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\npublic Optional<Booking> findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — null return leaks nullability into callers\npublic Booking findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id).orElse(null);\n}\n```\n\n### Use `Money` for All Monetary Values\n\nNever use `double`, `float`, or raw `BigDecimal` for monetary amounts.\nAlways use the `Money` value object from `shared/common/domain/Money`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nprivate final Money price;\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nprivate BigDecimal price;\nprivate double price;\n```\n\n### Logging\n\nUse SLF4J with `@Slf4j` (Lombok). Never use `System.out.println`.\nLog at the correct level:\n\n| Level | When to Use |\n|---|---|\n| `TRACE` | Verbose debug — method entry/exit during development only |\n| `DEBUG` | Internal state during a complex operation |\n| `INFO` | Significant business events: booking confirmed, payment received |\n| `WARN` | Recoverable issues: seat lock expired, retry attempted |\n| `ERROR` | Unrecoverable failures: payment charged but DB write failed |\n\nAlways include structured context. Use MDC for `traceId` and `requestId` (configured in `shared/config/TracingConfig`).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed. bookingId={} userId={} amount={}\", bookingId, userId, amount);\nlog.error(\"DB write failed after payment charge. bookingId={} paymentIntentId={}\", bookingId, paymentIntentId, e);\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nSystem.out.println(\"booking done\");\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed: \" + bookingId); // string concatenation, not parameterised\n```\n\n---\n\n## Inter-Module Communication Patterns\n\nModules communicate via REST (sync queries) or Spring Events (async commands). Never import another module's @Service directly (exception: `admin/` may import module `@Service` for READ-only operations).\n\n**Pattern 1: REST Query** — Module B needs data from upstream Module A\n```java\n// ✅ Call REST API\nprivate final RestClient restClient;\npublic Payment getPaymentDetails(UUID paymentId) {\n    return restClient.get(\"/api/payments/{id}\", paymentId, Payment.class);\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import @Service\nprivate final PaymentService paymentService; // WRONG\n```\n\n**Pattern 2: Spring Events** — Module A publishes, others listen\n```java\n// Module A publishes\neventPublisher.publishEvent(new PaymentConfirmedEvent(bookingId, amount));\n\n// Module B listens (delegates immediately to service)\n@Component\npublic class PaymentConfirmedListener {\n    @TransactionalEventListener(phase = TransactionPhase.AFTER_COMMIT)\n    public void onPaymentConfirmed(PaymentConfirmedEvent event) {\n        bookingService.confirmBooking(event.bookingId());\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never call service directly from event trigger\n// ❌ Never block the listener — delegate immediately\n```\n\n**Pattern 3: Shared Contracts** — Enums, DTOs, base classes in `shared/common/` only\n```java\n// ✅ Define once in shared/common/enums/\npublic enum BookingStatus { PENDING, CONFIRMED, CANCELLED, REFUNDED }\n\n// ❌ Never duplicate in multiple modules\n```\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facades — The Only Door to External Systems\n\n**HARD RULE: No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly. All calls go through shared/eventbrite/service/ facades only.**\n\n### The 10 Eventbrite Facades (All in `shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n| Facade | Used By | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| EbEventSyncService | discovery-catalog, scheduling, engagement | Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull catalog |\n| EbVenueService | scheduling | Create/update venues; list org venues |\n| EbScheduleService | scheduling | Create recurring event schedules |\n| EbTicketService | discovery-catalog, booking-inventory | Create/update ticket classes; check availability |\n| EbCapacityService | admin/ | Update event capacity tiers |\n| EbOrderService | payments-ticketing | Read orders post-checkout |\n| EbAttendeeService | payments-ticketing, engagement | Get attendees; verify attendance for reviews |\n| EbDiscountSyncService | promotions | Create/update/delete discount codes |\n| EbRefundService | payments-ticketing | Read refund status (no submission API) |\n| EbWebhookService | mock-eventbrite-api only | Mock/testing webhook registration and delivery simulation (NOT production) |\n\n### Correct Pattern\n\n```java\n// ✅ Inject facade, call it\n@Service\npublic class EventService {\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventService; // from shared/\n    \n    public Event createEvent(EventRequest request) {\n        Event event = new Event(request.name(), request.venue());\n        eventRepository.save(event); // save internally first\n        \n        EbEventResponse ebResp = ebEventService.createEvent(\n            mapper.toEbCreateRequest(event));\n        event.setEventbriteEventId(ebResp.id()); // store EB ID\n        eventRepository.save(event);\n        return event;\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```
