## Entry 250
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:58:54.506Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:58:54.506Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb351-0f46-73a2-8e09-a761f3e24fd4",
    "turn_id": "019cb353-38a5-7d93-87d8-527755cf467e",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb353-38a5-7d93-87d8-527755cf467e-plan",
      "text": "**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan With Spec Alignment (SPEC_2)\n\n**Summary**\nBuild the full Stage 8 test suite across `scheduling`, `discovery-catalog`, `booking-inventory`, `admin`, and `shared` with domain/service/api/arch/mapper coverage as specified in `POQ/Show_Venues_Events/SPEC_2.md`. In parallel, align production behavior where it materially diverges from SPEC_2 so tests can pass without weakening coverage.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (currently throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (currently throws/502).\n- `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/submit` should enforce DRAFT-only, pricing tiers present, and update error mapping per SPEC_2.\n- Validation constraints required in request DTOs for `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier with `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (currently in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve Before Tests**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition and guard validation methods required by Stage 8 domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain has no tier constraints; tests require FREE/PAID price/quota validation.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not check venue sync status, seatmap requirement, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict detection on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `ConflictDetectionService` returns only 1 same-venue alternative and 1 adjusted option, not “up to 3”; nearby venues use cityId string rather than city name.\n6. `SlotTicketSyncService` lacks idempotency per pricing tier, inventory tier creation mapping, and capacity/seatmap rules required by Stage 8.\n7. `OrgOAuthService` uses in-memory state store, not Redis TTL; error type differs from SPEC_2.\n8. `VenueService` EB failure handling returns exception rather than internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n9. `ShowSlotController` returns 200 on update and does not handle 207 in the contract.\n\n**Plan**\n1. Align production behavior to SPEC_2 where tests require it, using the gaps list above as the change checklist.\n2. Add shared test fixtures per module to minimize boilerplate and ensure AAA style:\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/ShowSlotFixtures.java`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/PricingTierFixtures.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/support/VenueFixtures.java`\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/support/OAuthFixtures.java`\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/support/OrgAuthFixtures.java`\n3. Implement **domain** tests for `scheduling`:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotTest.java\nTests:\n- should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_when_slot_is_DRAFT_and_has_pricing_tiers\n- should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_ACTIVE\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_submitting_non_DRAFT_slot\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_ACTIVE_to_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_CANCELLED_slot_to_any_state\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_ACTIVE_to_DRAFT\n- should_reset_syncAttemptCount_to_zero_when_slot_transitions_to_ACTIVE\n- should_increment_syncAttemptCount_when_recordSyncFailure_is_called\n- should_record_lastSyncError_and_lastAttemptedAt_when_recordSyncFailure_is_called\n- should_not_increment_syncAttemptCount_when_slot_reaches_ACTIVE\n- should_clear_lastSyncError_when_slot_transitions_to_ACTIVE\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_seatingMode_is_RESERVED_and_sourceSeatMapId_is_null\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_capacity_is_zero_or_negative\n- should_throw_IllegalArgumentException_when_endTime_is_not_after_startTime\n- should_allow_null_recurrenceRule_when_isRecurring_is_false\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_isRecurring_is_true_and_recurrenceRule_is_null\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_editing_time_fields_on_CANCELLED_slot\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_changing_seatingMode_on_ACTIVE_slot\n\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotPricingTierTest.java\nTests:\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_FREE_tier_has_non_zero_priceAmount\n- should_allow_zero_priceAmount_for_FREE_tier\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_quota_is_zero_or_negative\n- should_allow_PAID_tier_with_positive_priceAmount\n\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ConflictAlternativeResponseTest.java\nTests:\n- should_return_empty_lists_for_all_alternatives_when_no_options_found\n- should_construct_record_with_all_three_alternative_lists\n```\n4. Implement **service** tests:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotServiceTest.java\nTests:\n- should_create_slot_in_DRAFT_status_when_venue_is_synced_and_no_conflict\n- should_throw_SchedulingNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_via_rest_call\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_venue_sync_status_is_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_throw_SlotConflictException_with_alternatives_when_overlap_detected\n- should_throw_SlotConflictException_with_alternatives_when_gap_policy_violated\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_RESERVED_slot_has_no_sourceSeatMapId\n- should_persist_all_pricing_tiers_when_slot_created_successfully\n- should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_and_call_createDraft_when_slot_is_DRAFT\n- should_publish_SlotDraftCreatedEvent_with_correct_payload_when_EB_draft_succeeds\n- should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_not_publish_success_event_when_createDraft_fails\n- should_store_ebEventId_on_slot_immediately_after_createDraft_succeeds\n- should_transition_to_ACTIVE_and_reset_syncAttemptCount_when_publishEvent_succeeds\n- should_publish_ShowSlotActivatedEvent_with_correct_fields_when_slot_transitions_to_ACTIVE\n- should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_stay_PENDING_SYNC_when_publishEvent_fails\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_TicketSyncCompleted_arrives_for_non_PENDING_SYNC_slot\n- should_persist_update_and_call_updateEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE\n- should_persist_update_and_call_updateCapacityTier_when_capacity_changed_on_ACTIVE_slot\n- should_persist_internal_update_and_record_lastSyncError_when_updateEvent_fails\n- should_return_207_response_when_internal_update_succeeds_but_EB_update_fails\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_changing_seatingMode_on_ACTIVE_slot\n- should_rerun_conflict_check_with_excludeSlotId_when_time_fields_updated\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_and_call_cancelEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_even_when_cancelEvent_fails\n- should_publish_ShowSlotCancelledEvent_regardless_of_EB_cancel_outcome\n- should_record_lastSyncError_when_EB_cancel_fails_but_internal_cancellation_succeeds\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_cancelling_DRAFT_slot\n- should_skip_D1_and_attempt_publish_directly_when_eb_event_id_already_set\n- should_re_attempt_D1_when_eb_event_id_is_null_on_retry\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_retry_called_on_ACTIVE_slot\n- should_include_all_pricingTierIds_in_SlotDraftCreatedEvent\n- should_include_seatingMode_in_SlotDraftCreatedEvent\n- should_include_sourceSeatMapId_in_SlotDraftCreatedEvent_when_RESERVED\n- should_set_sourceSeatMapId_null_in_SlotDraftCreatedEvent_when_GA\n- should_not_include_any_Entity_object_in_SlotDraftCreatedEvent\n\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ConflictDetectionServiceTest.java\nTests:\n- should_detect_overlap_when_proposed_window_overlaps_existing_slot\n- should_detect_overlap_when_proposed_window_is_fully_contained_by_existing_slot\n- should_not_detect_conflict_when_slots_are_adjacent_with_no_gap\n- should_detect_gap_policy_violation_when_gap_between_slots_less_than_configured_minimum\n- should_not_detect_conflict_when_gap_is_exactly_the_minimum_required\n- should_exclude_self_from_conflict_check_when_updating_existing_slot\n- should_ignore_CANCELLED_slots_in_conflict_check\n- should_build_sameVenueAlternatives_with_up_to_3_non_conflicting_windows\n- should_build_nearbyVenueAlternatives_sorted_by_capacity_proximity\n- should_return_empty_nearbyVenueAlternatives_when_no_synced_venues_in_city\n- should_build_adjustedTimeOptions_using_max_end_time_plus_gap\n\nFile: booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java\nTests:\n- should_call_createTicketClasses_for_each_pricing_tier_and_store_eb_ticket_class_id\n- should_call_createInventoryTiers_after_createTicketClasses_succeeds\n- should_not_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_GA\n- should_call_copySeatMap_with_sourceSeatMapId_when_seatingMode_is_RESERVED\n- should_publish_TicketSyncCompletedEvent_when_all_D2_to_D5_steps_succeed\n- should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_with_step_identifier_when_D2_fails\n- should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_with_step_identifier_when_D3_fails\n- should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_with_step_identifier_when_D5_fails\n- should_skip_D2_for_tier_when_eb_ticket_class_id_already_populated_idempotency\n- should_skip_D3_for_tier_when_eb_inventory_tier_id_already_populated_idempotency\n- should_not_call_EbCapacityService_updateCapacityTier_during_initial_sync\n\nFile: discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/VenueServiceTest.java\nTests:\n- should_persist_venue_with_PENDING_SYNC_status_then_call_createVenue_on_EbVenueService\n- should_store_eb_venue_id_and_set_SYNCED_after_successful_EB_creation\n- should_persist_venue_and_return_202_when_EbVenueService_createVenue_throws\n- should_record_lastSyncError_when_EB_venue_creation_fails\n- should_update_venue_fields_then_call_updateVenue_on_EbVenueService_when_eb_venue_id_present\n- should_set_DRIFT_FLAGGED_and_publish_VenueDriftDetectedEvent_when_updateVenue_fails\n- should_always_invalidate_snapshot_cache_after_venue_update_regardless_of_EB_outcome\n- should_not_call_EbVenueService_on_update_when_eb_venue_id_is_null\n- should_throw_CatalogNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_by_id\n\nFile: discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/SlotActivatedListenerTest.java\nTests:\n- Capture results and write a test report under `test_result_folder/` using the required template and naming convention.\n\n**Test Cases and Scenarios**\nAll test cases listed above are copied directly from SPEC_2 Stage 8 and should be implemented verbatim unless a change is explicitly agreed upon.\n\n**Assumptions**\n- We will enforce SPEC_2 even when production code currently differs, and will plan code adjustments needed to satisfy tests.\n- API tests will use `@WebMvcTest` with `@WithMockUser(roles=\"ADMIN\")` for authorized requests and no auth header for 401 checks.\n- Failure events are allowed; tests will assert that success events are not emitted on failure but failure events may be emitted.\n\n"
```

```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb351-0f46-73a2-8e09-a761f3e24fd4",
    "turn_id": "019cb353-38a5-7d93-87d8-527755cf467e",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb353-38a5-7d93-87d8-527755cf467e-plan",
      "text": "**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan With Spec Alignment (SPEC_2)\n\n**Summary**\nBuild the full Stage 8 test suite across `scheduling`, `discovery-catalog`, `booking-inventory`, `admin`, and `shared` with domain/service/api/arch/mapper coverage as specified in `POQ/Show_Venues_Events/SPEC_2.md`. In parallel, align production behavior where it materially diverges from SPEC_2 so tests can pass without weakening coverage.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (currently throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (currently throws/502).\n- `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/submit` should enforce DRAFT-only, pricing tiers present, and update error mapping per SPEC_2.\n- Validation constraints required in request DTOs for `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier with `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (currently in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve Before Tests**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition and guard validation methods required by Stage 8 domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain has no tier constraints; tests require FREE/PAID price/quota validation.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not check venue sync status, seatmap requirement, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict detection on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `ConflictDetectionService` returns only 1 same-venue alternative and 1 adjusted option, not “up to 3”; nearby venues use cityId string rather than city name.\n6. `SlotTicketSyncService` lacks idempotency per pricing tier, inventory tier creation mapping, and capacity/seatmap rules required by Stage 8.\n7. `OrgOAuthService` uses in-memory state store, not Redis TTL; error type differs from SPEC_2.\n8. `VenueService` EB failure handling returns exception rather than internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n9. `ShowSlotController` returns 200 on update and does not handle 207 in the contract.\n\n**Plan**\n1. Align production behavior to SPEC_2 where tests require it, using the gaps list above as the change checklist.\n2. Add shared test fixtures per module to minimize boilerplate and ensure AAA style:\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/ShowSlotFixtures.java`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/PricingTierFixtures.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/support/VenueFixtures.java`\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/support/OAuthFixtures.java`\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/support/OrgAuthFixtures.java`\n3. Implement **domain** tests for `scheduling`:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotTest.java\nTests:\n- ```json
```
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:58:54.508Z",

    "type": "message",

    "content": [
      {
        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "<proposed_plan>\n**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan With Spec Alignment (SPEC_2)\n\n**Summary**\nBuild the full Stage 8 test suite across `scheduling`, `discovery-catalog`, `booking-inventory`, `admin`, and `shared` with domain/service/api/arch/mapper coverage as specified in `POQ/Show_Venues_Events/SPEC_2.md`. In parallel, align production behavior where it materially diverges from SPEC_2 so tests can pass without weakening coverage.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (currently throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (currently throws/502).\n- `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/submit` should enforce DRAFT-only, pricing tiers present, and update error mapping per SPEC_2.\n- Validation constraints required in request DTOs for `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier with `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (currently in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve Before Tests**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition and guard validation methods required by Stage 8 domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain has no tier constraints; tests require FREE/PAID price/quota validation.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not check venue sync status, seatmap requirement, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict detection on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `ConflictDetectionService` returns only 1 same-venue alternative and 1 adjusted option, not “up to 3”; nearby venues use cityId string rather than city name.\n6. `SlotTicketSyncService` lacks idempotency per pricing tier, inventory tier creation mapping, and capacity/seatmap rules required by Stage 8.\n7. `OrgOAuthService` uses in-memory state store, not Redis TTL; error type differs from SPEC_2.\n8. `VenueService` EB failure handling returns exception rather than internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n9. `ShowSlotController` returns 200 on update and does not handle 207 in the contract.\n\n**Plan**\n1. Align production behavior to SPEC_2 where tests require it, using the gaps list above as the change checklist.\n2. Add shared test fixtures per module to minimize boilerplate and ensure AAA style:\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/ShowSlotFixtures.java`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/PricingTierFixtures.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/support/VenueFixtures.java`\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/support/OAuthFixtures.java`\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/support/OrgAuthFixtures.java`\n3. Implement **domain** tests for `scheduling`:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotTest.java\nTests:\n- should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_when_slot_is_DRAFT_and_has_pricing_tiers\n- should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_ACTIVE\n- should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_submitting_non_DRAFT_slot\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_ACTIVE_to_PENDING_SYNC\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_CANCELLED_slot_to_any_state\n- should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_ACTIVE_to_DRAFT\n- should_reset_syncAttemptCount_to_zero_when_slot_transitions_to_ACTIVE\n-         "text": "<proposed_plan>\n**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan With Spec Alignment (SPEC_2)\n\n**Summary**\nBuild the full Stage 8 test suite across `scheduling`, `discovery-catalog`, `booking-inventory`, `admin`, and `shared` with domain/service/api/arch/mapper coverage as specified in `POQ/Show_Venues_Events/SPEC_2.md`. In parallel, align production behavior where it materially diverges from SPEC_2 so tests can pass without weakening coverage.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (currently throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (currently throws/502).\n- `POST /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/submit` should enforce DRAFT-only, pricing tiers present, and update error mapping per SPEC_2.\n- Validation constraints required in request DTOs for `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier with `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (currently in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve Before Tests**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition and guard validation methods required by Stage 8 domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain has no tier constraints; tests require FREE/PAID price/quota validation.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not check venue sync status, seatmap requirement, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict detection on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `ConflictDetectionService` returns only 1 same-venue alternative and 1 adjusted option, not “up to 3”; nearby venues use cityId string rather than city name.\n6. `SlotTicketSyncService` lacks idempotency per pricing tier, inventory tier creation mapping, and capacity/seatmap rules required by Stage 8.\n7. `OrgOAuthService` uses in-memory state store, not Redis TTL; error type differs from SPEC_2.\n8. `VenueService` EB failure handling returns exception rather than internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n9. `ShowSlotController` returns 200 on update and does not handle 207 in the contract.\n\n**Plan**\n1. Align production behavior to SPEC_2 where tests require it, using the gaps list above as the change checklist.\n2. Add shared test fixtures per module to minimize boilerplate and ensure AAA style:\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/ShowSlotFixtures.java`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/support/PricingTierFixtures.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/support/VenueFixtures.java`\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/support/OAuthFixtures.java`\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/support/OrgAuthFixtures.java`\n3. Implement **ArchUnit** additions per SPEC_2:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/arch/SchedulingArchTest.java\nFile: booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java\nFile: discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/arch/DiscoveryCatalogArchTest.java (extend existing)\nFile: shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/arch/SharedArchTest.java\n```\n7. Implement **Mapper** tests:\n```\nFile: scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/mapper/ShowSlotMapperTest.java\nFile: discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/mapper/VenueMapperTest.java\n```\n8. Execute tests in focused order, then module-level:\n- `mvn -pl scheduling -Dtest=*Test test`\n- `mvn -pl booking-inventory -Dtest=*Test test`\n- `mvn -pl discovery-catalog -Dtest=*Test test`\n- `mvn -pl admin -Dtest=*Test test`\n- `mvn -pl shared -Dtest=*Test test`\n9. Capture results and write a test report under `test_result_folder/` using the required template and naming convention.\n\n**Test Cases and Scenarios**\nAll test cases listed above are copied directly from SPEC_2 Stage 8 and should be implemented verbatim unless a change is explicitly agreed upon.\n\n**Assumptions**\n- We will enforce SPEC_2 even when production code currently differs, and will plan code adjustments needed to satisfy tests.\n- API tests will use `@WebMvcTest` with `@WithMockUser(roles=\"ADMIN\")` for authorized requests and no auth header for 401 checks.\n- Failure events are allowed; tests will assert that success events are not emitted on failure but failure events may be emitted.\n\n</proposed_plan>"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Entry 255

**Role/Type:** turn_context

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:59:23.428Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb35a-44a2-7333-9045-af2839ba9444",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
```json
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 268
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:01:18.992Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:01:18.992Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb35c-080e-7543-bbdf-ead7dd22489a",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```
