However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
```json
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
```
```json
    }
```
```json
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:01:57.051Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb351-0f46-73a2-8e09-a761f3e24fd4",
    "turn_id": "019cb35c-080e-7543-bbdf-ead7dd22489a",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb35c-080e-7543-bbdf-ead7dd22489a-plan",
      "text": "**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan (Trimmed: Core Flows + ArchUnit)\n\n**Summary**\nReduce test scope to core flows with balanced happy/negative coverage, keep ArchUnit boundary rules, and minimize mapper/domain edge cases. Omit reconciliation job tests and most mapper edge cases. Align production behavior where SPEC_2 and current implementation diverge so tests pass without weakening architecture rules.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (current behavior throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (current behavior throws/502).\n- Enforce validation constraints in request DTOs: `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier has `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (current in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve (Only Those Needed for This Plan)**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition guard/validation methods for basic domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain lacks tier constraints.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not enforce venue sync status, RESERVED seatmap, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict check on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `VenueService.createVenue` returns exception on EB failure instead of internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n6. `OrgOAuthService` state handling differs from SPEC_2 error types.\n\n**Test Scope (Reduced, Happy/Negative Balanced)**\n\n**1) Domain Tests (Minimal Core)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_when_slot_is_DRAFT_and_has_pricing_tiers`\n    - `should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_submitting_non_DRAFT_slot`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_CANCELLED_slot_to_any_state`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotPricingTierTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_allow_zero_priceAmount_for_FREE_tier`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_FREE_tier_has_non_zero_priceAmount`\n\n**2) Service Tests (Core Flows)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_create_slot_in_DRAFT_status_when_venue_is_synced_and_no_conflict`\n    - `should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_and_call_createDraft_when_slot_is_DRAFT`\n    - `should_publish_SlotDraftCreatedEvent_with_correct_payload_when_EB_draft_succeeds`\n    - `should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_publishEvent_succeeds`\n    - `should_persist_update_and_call_updateEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_and_call_cancelEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_SchedulingNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_via_rest_call`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_venue_sync_status_is_PENDING_SYNC`\n    - `should_throw_SlotConflictException_with_alternatives_when_overlap_detected`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_RESERVED_slot_has_no_sourceSeatMapId`\n    - `should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_not_publish_success_event_when_createDraft_fails`\n    - `should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_stay_PENDING_SYNC_when_publishEvent_fails`\n    - `should_persist_internal_update_and_return_207_when_EB_update_fails`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_even_when_cancelEvent_fails`\n\n- `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_call_createTicketClasses_and_inventoryTiers_when_syncing`\n    - `should_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_RESERVED`\n    - `should_publish_TicketSyncCompletedEvent_when_all_steps_succeed`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_when_ticket_class_creation_fails`\n    - `should_not_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_GA`\n\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/VenueServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_persist_venue_and_set_SYNCED_when_EB_creation_succeeds`\n    - `should_update_venue_and_call_updateVenue_when_eb_venue_id_present`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_persist_venue_and_return_202_when_EB_create_fails`\n    - `should_set_DRIFT_FLAGGED_when_updateVenue_fails`\n    - `should_throw_CatalogNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_by_id`\n\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/service/OrgOAuthServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_generate_authorization_url_and_store_csrf_state`\n    - `should_call_exchangeAndStore_and_return_CONNECTED_when_code_and_state_valid`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_ValidationException_when_csrf_state_invalid_or_expired`\n\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/eventbrite/service/EbTokenStoreTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_decrypted_access_token_when_token_valid`\n    - `should_update_token_and_return_new_access_token_after_successful_refresh`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_set_status_TOKEN_EXPIRED_and_throw_EbAuthException_when_refresh_fails`\n    - `should_throw_EbAuthException_immediately_when_status_is_REVOKED`\n\n**3) API Tests (Core Endpoints Only)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/api/ShowSlotControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_slot_created`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_submitted`\n    - `should_return_200_when_active_slot_updated`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_cancelled`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_endTime_is_not_after_startTime`\n    - `should_return_422_when_conflict_detected`\n    - `should_return_502_when_createDraft_fails`\n    - `should_return_207_when_internal_update_succeeds_but_EB_sync_fails`\n    - `should_return_422_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot`\n    - `should_return_401_when_no_auth_token_provided`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/api/VenueAdminControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_venue_created_and_EB_synced`\n    - `should_return_200_when_venue_updated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_202_when_venue_created_but_EB_sync_failed`\n    - `should_return_400_when_name_blank`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/OrgEventbriteControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_200_with_authorization_url_when_connect_initiated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_oauth_state_invalid`\n    - `should_return_502_when_code_exchange_fails`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n**4) ArchUnit Tests (Keep)**\n- `scheduling/arch/SchedulingArchTest.java`  \n- `booking-inventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`  \n- `discovery-catalog/arch/DiscoveryCatalogArchTest.java` (extend existing)  \n- `shared/arch/SharedArchTest.java`  \n\n**Execution Order**\n1. Implement/adjust production code for gaps listed above.\n2. Add tests in the order: domain → service → api → arch.\n3. Run module test suites:\n   - `mvn -pl scheduling -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl booking-inventory -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl discovery-catalog -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl admin -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl shared -Dtest=*Test test`\n4. Write result log in `test_result_folder/` using required template.\n\n**Test Cases and Scenarios**\nAll listed tests above are the reduced core set, balanced happy/negative.\n\n**Assumptions**\n- Failure events are allowed; tests will assert no success event on failure, but failure events may be published.\n- Security tests use `@WithMockUser(roles = \"ADMIN\")` for authorized calls and omit auth for 401 checks.\n- If any endpoint returns a different HTTP code than SPEC_2, production code will be aligned (not the tests).\n\n"
    }
  }
}
```

```json
      "text": "**Title**\nFR2 Stage 8 Test Plan (Trimmed: Core Flows + ArchUnit)\n\n**Summary**\nReduce test scope to core flows with balanced happy/negative coverage, keep ArchUnit boundary rules, and minimize mapper/domain edge cases. Omit reconciliation job tests and most mapper edge cases. Align production behavior where SPEC_2 and current implementation diverge so tests pass without weakening architecture rules.\n\n**Important API / Interface Changes To Align With SPEC_2**\n- `POST /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues` should return `202 Accepted` when EB sync fails but internal create succeeds (current behavior throws/502).\n- `PUT /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}` should return `207 Multi-Status` when EB update fails after internal update (current behavior throws/502).\n- Enforce validation constraints in request DTOs: `endTime > startTime`, `capacity > 0`, `quota > 0`, `priceAmount >= 0`, `FREE` tier has `priceAmount=0`, `sourceSeatMapId` required for RESERVED.\n- OAuth state storage should be TTL-based and mapped to `ValidationException` per SPEC_2 (current in-memory map and `IllegalArgumentException`).\n\n**Spec vs Implementation Gaps To Resolve (Only Those Needed for This Plan)**\n1. `ShowSlot` domain lacks transition guard/validation methods for basic domain tests.\n2. `ShowSlotPricingTier` domain lacks tier constraints.\n3. `ShowSlotService.createSlot` does not enforce venue sync status, RESERVED seatmap, or pricing tier rules.\n4. `ShowSlotService.updateSlot` does not block `PENDING_SYNC`, does not rerun conflict check on time changes, and does not return `207`.\n5. `VenueService.createVenue` returns exception on EB failure instead of internal success with `PENDING_SYNC`.\n6. `OrgOAuthService` state handling differs from SPEC_2 error types.\n\n**Test Scope (Reduced, Happy/Negative Balanced)**\n\n**1) Domain Tests (Minimal Core)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_when_slot_is_DRAFT_and_has_pricing_tiers`\n    - `should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_slot_is_PENDING_SYNC`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_submitting_non_DRAFT_slot`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_transitioning_CANCELLED_slot_to_any_state`\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotPricingTierTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_allow_zero_priceAmount_for_FREE_tier`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_FREE_tier_has_non_zero_priceAmount`\n\n**2) Service Tests (Core Flows)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_create_slot_in_DRAFT_status_when_venue_is_synced_and_no_conflict`\n    - `should_transition_to_PENDING_SYNC_and_call_createDraft_when_slot_is_DRAFT`\n    - `should_publish_SlotDraftCreatedEvent_with_correct_payload_when_EB_draft_succeeds`\n    - `should_transition_to_ACTIVE_when_publishEvent_succeeds`\n    - `should_persist_update_and_call_updateEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_and_call_cancelEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_SchedulingNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_via_rest_call`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_venue_sync_status_is_PENDING_SYNC`\n    - `should_throw_SlotConflictException_with_alternatives_when_overlap_detected`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_RESERVED_slot_has_no_sourceSeatMapId`\n    - `should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_not_publish_success_event_when_createDraft_fails`\n    - `should_increment_syncAttemptCount_and_stay_PENDING_SYNC_when_publishEvent_fails`\n    - `should_persist_internal_update_and_return_207_when_EB_update_fails`\n    - `should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot`\n    - `should_transition_to_CANCELLED_even_when_cancelEvent_fails`\n\n- `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_call_createTicketClasses_and_inventoryTiers_when_syncing`\n    - `should_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_RESERVED`\n    - `should_publish_TicketSyncCompletedEvent_when_all_steps_succeed`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_when_ticket_class_creation_fails`\n    - `should_not_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_GA`\n\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/VenueServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_persist_venue_and_set_SYNCED_when_EB_creation_succeeds`\n    - `should_update_venue_and_call_updateVenue_when_eb_venue_id_present`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_persist_venue_and_return_202_when_EB_create_fails`\n    - `should_set_DRIFT_FLAGGED_when_updateVenue_fails`\n    - `should_throw_CatalogNotFoundException_when_venue_not_found_by_id`\n\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/service/OrgOAuthServiceTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_generate_authorization_url_and_store_csrf_state`\n    - `should_call_exchangeAndStore_and_return_CONNECTED_when_code_and_state_valid`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_throw_ValidationException_when_csrf_state_invalid_or_expired`\n\n- `shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/eventbrite/service/EbTokenStoreTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_decrypted_access_token_when_token_valid`\n    - `should_update_token_and_return_new_access_token_after_successful_refresh`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_set_status_TOKEN_EXPIRED_and_throw_EbAuthException_when_refresh_fails`\n    - `should_throw_EbAuthException_immediately_when_status_is_REVOKED`\n\n**3) API Tests (Core Endpoints Only)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/api/ShowSlotControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_slot_created`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_submitted`\n    - `should_return_200_when_active_slot_updated`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_cancelled`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_endTime_is_not_after_startTime`\n    - `should_return_422_when_conflict_detected`\n    - `should_return_502_when_createDraft_fails`\n    - `should_return_207_when_internal_update_succeeds_but_EB_sync_fails`\n    - `should_return_422_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot`\n    - `should_return_401_when_no_auth_token_provided`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/api/VenueAdminControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_venue_created_and_EB_synced`\n    - `should_return_200_when_venue_updated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_202_when_venue_created_but_EB_sync_failed`\n    - `should_return_400_when_name_blank`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/OrgEventbriteControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_200_with_authorization_url_when_connect_initiated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_oauth_state_invalid`\n    - `should_return_502_when_code_exchange_fails`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n**4) ArchUnit Tests (Keep)**\n- `scheduling/arch/SchedulingArchTest.java`  \n- `booking-inventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`  \n- `discovery-catalog/arch/DiscoveryCatalogArchTest.java` (extend existing)  \n- `shared/arch/SharedArchTest.java`  \n\n**Execution Order**\n1. Implement/adjust production code for gaps listed above.\n2. Add tests in the order: domain → service → api → arch.\n3. Run module test suites:\n   - `mvn -pl scheduling -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl booking-inventory -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl discovery-catalog -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl admin -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl shared -Dtest=*Test test`\n4. Write result log in `test_result_folder/` using required template.\n\n**Test Cases and Scenarios**\nAll listed tests above are the reduced core set, balanced happy/negative.\n\n**Assumptions**\n- Failure events are allowed; tests will assert no success event on failure, but failure events may be published.\n- Security tests use `@WithMockUser(roles = \"ADMIN\")` for authorized calls and omit auth for 401 checks.\n- If any endpoint returns a different HTTP code than SPEC_2, production code will be aligned (not the tests).\n\n"
    }
  }
}
```
---

`should_throw_EbAuthException_immediately_when_status_is_REVOKED`\n\n**3) API Tests (Core Endpoints Only)**\n- `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/api/ShowSlotControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_slot_created`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_submitted`\n    - `should_return_200_when_active_slot_updated`\n    - `should_return_200_when_slot_cancelled`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_endTime_is_not_after_startTime`\n    - `should_return_422_when_conflict_detected`\n    - `should_return_502_when_createDraft_fails`\n    - `should_return_207_when_internal_update_succeeds_but_EB_sync_fails`\n    - `should_return_422_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot`\n    - `should_return_401_when_no_auth_token_provided`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `discovery-catalog/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/api/VenueAdminControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_201_when_venue_created_and_EB_synced`\n    - `should_return_200_when_venue_updated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_202_when_venue_created_but_EB_sync_failed`\n    - `should_return_400_when_name_blank`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n- `admin/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/OrgEventbriteControllerTest.java`\n  - Happy:\n    - `should_return_200_with_authorization_url_when_connect_initiated`\n  - Negative:\n    - `should_return_400_when_oauth_state_invalid`\n    - `should_return_502_when_code_exchange_fails`\n    - `should_return_403_when_user_not_ADMIN`\n\n**4) ArchUnit Tests (Keep)**\n- `scheduling/arch/SchedulingArchTest.java`  \n- `booking-inventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`  \n- `discovery-catalog/arch/DiscoveryCatalogArchTest.java` (extend existing)  \n- `shared/arch/SharedArchTest.java`  \n\n**Execution Order**\n1. Implement/adjust production code for gaps listed above.\n2. Add tests in the order: domain → service → api → arch.\n3. Run module test suites:\n   - `mvn -pl scheduling -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl booking-inventory -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl discovery-catalog -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl admin -Dtest=*Test test`\n   - `mvn -pl shared -Dtest=*Test test`\n4. Write result log in `test_result_folder/` using required template.\n\n**Test Cases and Scenarios**\nAll listed tests above are the reduced core set, balanced happy/negative.\n\n**Assumptions**\n- Failure events are allowed; tests will assert no success event on failure, but failure events may be published.\n- Security tests use `@WithMockUser(roles = \"ADMIN\")` for authorized calls and omit auth for 401 checks.\n- If any endpoint returns a different HTTP code than SPEC_2, production code will be aligned (not the tests).\n\n</proposed_plan>"
```json
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Entry 277
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:03:50.154Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:03:50.154Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb35e-5688-74a1-88d4-5f363992a6de",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
```json
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 286
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:04:05.363Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:04:05.363Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_H3jdqnGxbv2xu43K6o0mClbD",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 66668c\nWall time: 0.0531 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 250\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.scheduling.statemachine;\n\nimport java.util.EnumMap;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\n\n@Component\npublic class ShowSlotStateMachineConfig {\n\n    private final Map<ShowSlotState, Map<ShowSlotEvent, ShowSlotState>> transitions = new EnumMap<>(ShowSlotState.class);\n\n    public ShowSlotStateMachineConfig() {\n        transitions.put(ShowSlotState.DRAFT, Map.of(ShowSlotEvent.SUBMIT, ShowSlotState.PENDING_SYNC));\n        transitions.put(ShowSlotState.PENDING_SYNC, Map.of(\n            ShowSlotEvent.EB_PUBLISHED, ShowSlotState.ACTIVE,\n            ShowSlotEvent.EB_FAILED, ShowSlotState.PENDING_SYNC\n        ));\n        transitions.put(ShowSlotState.ACTIVE, Map.of(ShowSlotEvent.CANCEL, ShowSlotState.CANCELLED));\n        transitions.put(ShowSlotState.CANCELLED, Map.of());\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlotState nextState(ShowSlotState current, ShowSlotEvent event) {\n        return transitions.getOrDefault(current, Map.of()).get(event);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

```json
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
```
```json
    "text": "**Planning domain transition enforcement**\n\nThe domain transition methods currently lack validations, so adding checks inside ShowSlotStateMachineService before actions makes sense to enforce rules. To keep domain tests pure, implementing domain methods that validate transitions directly in ShowSlot will allow tests to invoke these safely, and the state machine can depend on them to maintain consistency."
  }
}
```

org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClientResponseException;\n\n@Service\npublic class VenueCatalogClient {\n\n    private final RestClient restClient;\n\n    public VenueCatalogClient(@Value(\"${app.internal-base-url:http://localhost:8080}\") String baseUrl) {\n        this.restClient = RestClient.builder().baseUrl(baseUrl).build();\n    }\n\n    public CatalogVenueResponse getVenue(Long venueId) {\n        try {\n            return restClient.get()\n                .uri(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues/{id}\", venueId)\n                .retrieve()\n                .body(CatalogVenueResponse.class);\n        } catch (RestClientResponseException ex) {\n            if (ex.getStatusCode() == HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND) {\n catalog\", \"CATALOG_INTEGRATION_ERROR\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    public List<CatalogVenueResponse> listVenuesByCity(Long cityId, Long organizationId) {\n        try {\n            CatalogVenueListResponse response = restClient.get()\n                .uri(uriBuilder -> uriBuilder\n                    .path(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\")\n                    .queryParam(\"cityId\", return response == null || response.venues() == null ? List.of() : response.venues();\n        } catch (RestClientResponseException ex) {\n            throw new IntegrationException(\"Failed to fetch venues from catalog\", \"CATALOG_INTEGRATION_ERROR\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n"

```json
{
```

```json
    "text": "**Extending venue response and validation**\n\nPlanning to add syncStatus and possibly eventbriteVenueId to the CatalogVenueResponse and update related records to track sync state.   }
}
```

```json
    "output": "Chunk ID: 1498ca\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 396\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception;\n\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.MethodArgumentNotValidException;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ExceptionHandler;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestControllerAdvice;\n\n@RestControllerAdvice\npublic class GlobalExceptionHandler {\n\n    @ExceptionHandler(BaseException.class)\n    public ResponseEntity<ErrorResponse> handleBaseException(BaseException ex) {\n        ErrorResponse response = new ErrorResponse(\n            ex.getErrorCode(),\n            ex.getMessage(),\n            ex.getHttpStatus().value(),\n            Instant.now(),\n            ex.getDetails()\n        );\n        return ResponseEntity.status(ex.getHttpStatus()).body(response);\n    }\n\n    @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class)\n    public ResponseEntity<ErrorResponse> handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex) {\n        ErrorResponse response = new ErrorResponse(\n            \"VALIDATION_ERROR\",\n            ex.getMessage(),\n            400,\n            Instant.now(),\n            null\n        );\n        return ResponseEntity.badRequest().body(response);\n    }\n\n    @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class)\n    public ResponseEntity<ErrorResponse> handleUnexpected(Exception ex) {\n        ErrorResponse response = new ErrorResponse(\n            \"INTERNAL_ERROR\",\n            ex.getMessage(),\n            500,\n            Instant.now(),\n            null\n        );\n        return ResponseEntity.status(500).body(response);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

```json
}
```

## Entry 333

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:05:38.720Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Deciding domain method use and state machine integration**\n\nI've concluded to add domain methods for transitions with validations used primarily in domain tests, while keeping the state machine service’s guard and action logic mostly unchanged for production flow. I'll implement domain validations for pricing tiers, especially validating free tiers' prices, and adjust domain tests accordingly without modifying core state machine behavior."
  }
}
```

com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.dto.response.ShowSlotPricingTierResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.dto.response.ShowSlotResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.dto.response.TimeWindowOptionDto;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.dto.response.VenueOptionDto;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlot;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlotOccurrence;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlotPricingTier;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.enums.TierType;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.value.ConflictAlternativeResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.value.TimeWindowOption;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.value.VenueOption;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.Money;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapper;\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapping;\n\n@Mapper(componentModel = \"spring\")\npublic interface ShowSlotMapper {\n\n    @Mapping(target = \"pricingTiers\", expression = \"java(mapPricingTiers(slot.getPricingTiers()))\")\n    ShowSlotResponse toResponse(ShowSlot slot);\n\n    ShowSlotOccurrenceResponse toOccurrenceResponse(ShowSlotOccurrence occurrence);\n\n    @Mapping(target = \"priceAmount\", source = \"price.amount\")\n    @Mapping(target = \"currency\", source = \"price.currency\")\n    ShowSlotPricingTierResponse toPricingTierResponse(ShowSlotPricingTier tier);\n\n    default List<ShowSlotPricingTierResponse> mapPricingTiers(List<ShowSlotPricingTier> tiers) {\n        return tiers == null ? List.of() : tiers.stream().map(this::toPricingTierResponse).toList();\n    }\n\n    default ShowSlot toEntity(Long organizationId, Long venueId, Long cityId, CreateShowSlotRequest request) {\n        return new ShowSlot(\n            organizationId,\n            venueId,\n            cityId,\n            request.title(),\n            request.description(),\n            request.startTime(),\n            request.endTime(),\n            request.seatingMode(),\n            request.capacity(),\n            Boolean.TRUE.equals(request.isRecurring()),\n            request.recurrenceRule(),\n            request.sourceSeatMapId()\n        );\n    }\n\n    default List<ShowSlotPricingTier> toPricingTiers(List<PricingTierRequest> tiers) {\n        if (tiers == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return tiers.stream().map(this::toPricingTier).toList();\n    }\n\n    default ShowSlotPricingTier toPricingTier(PricingTierRequest request) {\n        Money price = new Money(request.priceAmount(), request.currency());\n        TierType type = request.tierType();\n        return new ShowSlotPricingTier(request.name(), price, request.quota(), type);\n    }\n\n    default ConflictAlternativeDto toConflictDto(ConflictAlternativeResponse response) {\n        return new ConflictAlternativeDto(\n            mapTimeWindows(response.sameVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapVenues(response.nearbyVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapTimeWindows(response.adjustedTimeOptions())\n        );\n    }\n\n    default List<TimeWindowOptionDto> mapTimeWindows(List<TimeWindowOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toTimeWindowDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default TimeWindowOptionDto toTimeWindowDto(TimeWindowOption option) {\n        return new TimeWindowOptionDto(option.proposedStart(), option.proposedEnd());\n    }\n\n    default List<VenueOptionDto> mapVenues(List<VenueOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toVenueDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default VenueOptionDto toVenueDto(VenueOption option) {\n        return new VenueOptionDto(option.venueId(), option.venueName(), option.city(), option.capacity());\n    }\n}\n"
```json
  }
}
```

## Entry 349
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:06:24.113Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:06:24.113Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Resolving validation vs business exceptions**\n\nNoticed an inconsistency: service tests expect BusinessRuleException for reserved seat map missing, but API tests expect 400 (ValidationException). Need to align by either converting service exception to ValidationException or adjusting API tests to accept 422 error."
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Resolving validation vs business exceptions**\n\nNoticed an inconsistency: service tests expect BusinessRuleException for reserved seat map missing, but API tests expect 400 (ValidationException). Need to align by either converting service exception to ValidationException or adjusting API tests to accept 422 error."
  }
}
```
---
## Entry 350
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:06:24.115Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:06:24.115Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Aligning exception types and adjusting API status handling**\n\nDecided to replace BusinessRuleException with ValidationException in service to keep 400 for validation errors, adjusting tests accordingly. Planning to modify venue creation/update to quietly handle EB integration failures—returning 202 or 200 with drift flags—by changing service methods and controller responses to reflect sync status without throwing exceptions. For slot updates, will introduce a wrapper result to convey EB sync failure and return 207 status while maintaining existing response data. Also updating venue catalog response to include eventbriteVenueId (avoiding cross-module imports) to infer sync state in scheduling client and conflict detection."
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json

Planning to modify venue creation/update to quietly handle EB integration failures—returning 202 or 200 with drift flags—by changing service methods and controller responses to reflect sync status without throwing exceptions. For slot updates, will introduce a wrapper result to convey EB sync failure and return 207 status while maintaining existing response data. Also updating venue catalog response to include eventbriteVenueId (avoiding cross-module imports) to infer sync state in scheduling client and conflict detection."
  }
}
```

```json
    "output": "Chunk ID: 09bd47\nWall time: 0.0514 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 425\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlot;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.enums.ShowSlotStatus;\nimport java.time.ZonedDateTime;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.Page;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.Pageable;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaSpecificationExecutor;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.Query;\nimport org.springframework.data.repository.query.Param;\n\npublic interface ShowSlotRepository extends JpaRepository<ShowSlot, Long>, JpaSpecificationExecutor<ShowSlot> {\n\n    @Query(\"select s from ShowSlot s where s.venueId = :venueId and s.status <> 'CANCELLED' and s.startTime < :endTime and s.endTime > :startTime\")\n    List<ShowSlot> findConflicts(\n        @Param(\"venueId\") Long venueId,\n        @Param(\"startTime\") ZonedDateTime startTime,\n        @Param(\"endTime\") ZonedDateTime endTime\n    );\n\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findFirstByVenueIdAndEndTimeLessThanEqualOrderByEndTimeDesc(Long venueId, ZonedDateTime startTime);\n\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findFirstByVenueIdAndStartTimeGreaterThanEqualOrderByStartTimeAsc(Long venueId, ZonedDateTime endTime);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatus(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Pageable pageable);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndSyncAttemptCountGreaterThan(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, int attempts, Pageable pageable);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndLastSyncErrorIsNotNull(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Pageable pageable);\n}\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 365
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T11:07:36.640Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:07:36.640Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Extending conflict detection for updates**\n\nPlanning to add exclude slotId support in conflict checks by filtering conflicting slots after repository calls, updating gap policy checks to skip the slot with excludeId, and overloading validation to accept optional excludeSlotId for updates affecting start/end times. Also refining alternative venue options to follow spec with filtering by eventbriteVenueId and adjusting city name sourcing."
  }
}
```

## Entry 366

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:07:36.643Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Detailing update validation and conflict logic**\n\nPlanning extensive ShowSlot update to rerun conflict detection on time changes with exclude slotId support, validate venue sync via eventbriteVenueId on creation, add transition methods enforcing state rules, and implement ShowSlotUpdateResult to capture Eventbrite sync failures without throwing. Also refining conflict repository queries to exclude specific slots and enhance alternative venue selection per spec."
  }
}
```

**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:07:43.239Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Updating failure status handling and planning OAuth service tests**\n\nThe venue update failure status will switch from marking sync failed to marking drift as flagged per spec. OAuth service will throw a ValidationException on state validation failure and implement TTL in an in-memory state store, with plans to add focused JUnit5/Mockito tests concentrating on core flows."
  }
}
```

```json
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlot.java\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.enums.ShowSlotStatus;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.enums.SeatingMode;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\n import jakarta.persistence.CascadeType;\n@@\n     public void addOccurrence(ShowSlotOccurrence occurrence) {\n         occurrences.add(occurrence);\n         occurrence.attachTo(this);\n     }\n+\n+    public void submit() {\n+        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> PENDING_SYNC\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n+        }\n+        if (pricingTiers == null || pricingTiers.isEmpty()) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot must have at least one pricing tier before submission\", \"MISSING_PRICING_TIERS\");\n+        }\n+        markPendingSync();\n+    }\n+\n+    public void activate() {\n+        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> ACTIVE\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n+        }\n+        markActive();\n+    }\n+\n+    public void cancel() {\n+        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot already cancelled\", \"SLOT_ALREADY_CANCELLED\");\n+        }\n+        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"DRAFT slots must be deleted, not cancelled\", \"SLOT_DRAFT_CANCEL\");\n+        }\n+        markCancelled();\n+    }\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

```json
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/domain/ShowSlotPricingTier.java\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.enums.TierType;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.Money;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\n import jakarta.persistence.AttributeOverride;\n@@\n     protected ShowSlotPricingTier() {\n     }\n \n     public ShowSlotPricingTier(String name, Money price, Integer quota, TierType tierType) {\n+        if (quota == null || quota <= 0) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Pricing tier quota must be greater than 0\", \"INVALID_TIER_QUOTA\");\n+        }\n+        if (tierType == TierType.FREE && price != null && price.amount().signum() > 0) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"FREE tier must have priceAmount = 0\", \"INVALID_TIER_PRICE\");\n+        }\n         this.name = name;\n         this.price = price;\n         this.quota = quota;\n         this.tierType = tierType;\n     }\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T11:08:27.474Z",

    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_qGeGUPSDp2tYfPp5wLrfxATw",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotService.java\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.scheduling.statemachine.ShowSlotEvent;\n import com.eventplatform.scheduling.statemachine.ShowSlotStateMachineService;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.ShowSlotActivatedEvent;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.ShowSlotCancelledEvent;\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.IntegrationException;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.ValidationException;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.EbEventCreateRequest;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.EbEventDto;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.EbEventUpdateRequest;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.EbScheduleResponse;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbCapacityService;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbEventSyncService;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbScheduleService;\n+import java.math.BigDecimal;\n import java.time.Instant;\n import java.util.List;\n import java.util.Optional;\n@@\n     @Transactional\n     public ShowSlot createSlot(Long organizationId, CreateShowSlotRequest request) {\n         CatalogVenueResponse venue = venueCatalogClient.getVenue(request.venueId());\n+        validateCreateRequest(venue, request);\n         conflictDetectionService.validateOrThrow(organizationId, venue, request.startTime(), request.endTime());\n \n         ShowSlot slot = showSlotMapper.toEntity(organizationId, request.venueId(), venue.cityId(), request);\n         List<ShowSlotPricingTier> tiers = showSlotMapper.toPricingTiers(request.pricingTiers());\n         tiers.forEach(slot::addPricingTier);\n@@\n         return slot;\n     }\n@@\n-    public ShowSlot updateSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId, UpdateShowSlotRequest request) {\n+    public ShowSlotUpdateResult updateSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId, UpdateShowSlotRequest request) {\n         ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\n         if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\n             throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Cannot update a cancelled slot\", \"SLOT_CANCELLED\");\n         }\n+        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot cannot be edited while sync is in progress (PENDING_SYNC)\", \"SLOT_PENDING_SYNC\");\n+        }\n+\n+        if (request.startTime() != null || request.endTime() != null) {\n+            CatalogVenueResponse venue = venueCatalogClient.getVenue(slot.getVenueId());\n+            conflictDetectionService.validateOrThrow(organizationId, venue, request.startTime(), request.endTime(), slot.getId());\n+        }\n \n         Integer previousCapacity = slot.getCapacity();\n         slot.updateDetails(request.title(), request.description(), request.startTime(), request.endTime(), request.capacity());\n \n         if (request.pricingTiers() != null) {\n+            validatePricingTiers(request.pricingTiers());\n             slot.clearPricingTiers();\n             showSlotMapper.toPricingTiers(request.pricingTiers()).forEach(slot::addPricingTier);\n         }\n \n         slot = showSlotRepository.save(slot);\n \n+        boolean ebSyncFailed = false;\n         if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE) {\n             try {\n                 ebEventSyncService.updateEvent(organizationId, slot.getEbEventId(), toEbUpdateRequest(slot));\n                 if (request.capacity() != null && !request.capacity().equals(previousCapacity)) {\n                     ebCapacityService.updateCapacityTier(slot.getEbEventId(), request.capacity());\n                 }\n             } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n                 slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\n                 showSlotRepository.save(slot);\n-                throw ex;\n+                ebSyncFailed = true;\n             }\n         }\n \n-        return slot;\n+        return new ShowSlotUpdateResult(slot, ebSyncFailed);\n     }\n@@\n     private EbEventCreateRequest toEbCreateRequest(ShowSlot slot) {\n         return new EbEventCreateRequest(\n             slot.getTitle(),\n             slot.getDescription(),\n@@\n         );\n     }\n \n     private EbEventUpdateRequest toEbUpdateRequest(ShowSlot slot) {\n         return new EbEventUpdateRequest(\n             slot.getTitle(),\n             slot.getDescription(),\n             slot.getStartTime(),\n             slot.getEndTime()\n         );\n     }\n+\n+    private void validateCreateRequest(CatalogVenueResponse venue, CreateShowSlotRequest request) {\n+        if (venue.eventbriteVenueId() == null || venue.eventbriteVenueId().isBlank()) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Cannot create slot for venue not yet synced to Eventbrite\", \"VENUE_NOT_SYNCED\");\n+        }\n+        if (request.seatingMode() != null && request.seatingMode().name().equals(\"RESERVED\")\n+            && (request.sourceSeatMapId() == null || request.sourceSeatMapId().isBlank())) {\n+            throw new ValidationException(\"sourceSeatMapId is required for RESERVED seating\", \"SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR\");\n+        }\n+        validatePricingTiers(request.pricingTiers());\n+    }\n+\n+    private void validatePricingTiers(List<CreateShowSlotRequest.PricingTierRequest> tiers) {\n+    }\n+\n+    private void validatePricingTiers(List<com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.dto.request.PricingTierRequest> tiers) {\n+        if (tiers == null || tiers.isEmpty()) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot must have at least one pricing tier before submission\", \"MISSING_PRICING_TIERS\");\n+        }\n+        for (var tier : tiers) {\n+            if (tier.quota() == null || tier.quota() <= 0) {\n+                throw new ValidationException(\"quota must be greater than 0\", \"SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR\");\n+            }\n+            if (tier.priceAmount() == null || tier.priceAmount().compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) < 0) {\n+                throw new ValidationException(\"priceAmount must be non-negative\", \"SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR\");\n+            }\n+            if (tier.tierType() != null && tier.tierType().name().equals(\"FREE\")\n+                && tier.priceAmount().compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) > 0) {\n+                throw new ValidationException(\"FREE tier must have priceAmount = 0\", \"SLOT_VALIDATION_ERROR\");\n+            }\n+        }\n+    }\n }\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```
